_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

The user is saying that the mod loads via hot-reload (F6) after the main menu, not at game startup. This is important because:

1. The Harmony patches are applied when the mod loads
2. If the mod loads after the Stationpedia has already been initialized, certain things might not work correctly
3. The history might already be in a certain state

The user wants to be able to hot-reload and test changes without restarting the game each time.

Let me think about what might be going wrong:

1. **Button sizing issue**: The buttons are cloned from the vanilla buttons. If the vanilla buttons haven't been properly initialized when our mod loads (via hot-reload), we might be copying incomplete data.

2. **History issue**: When the mod hot-reloads, the Stationpedia might already have a history state. Our changes to history tracking might not be taking effect properly.

3. **The cleanup might not be working correctly**: When hot-reloading, we need to properly clean up and re-initialize.

Let me check the hot-reload / cleanup code and see if there's an issue with how we're initializing the HomePageLayoutManager.

The key issue is that when hot-reloading:
1. The mod's `Cleanup()` method is called
2. Then the mod re-initializes
3. But the Stationpedia UI state might persist

For the buttons, the issue might be that when we clone buttons during hot-reload, the parent layout group has already calculated its layout, and our new buttons don't trigger a re-layout.

Let me suggest some fixes:
1. Force layout rebuild after adding buttons
2. Log more information about what's happening during button creation
3. Make sure we're properly cleaning up and re-initializing

Also, for the history issue - if the mod loads after the user has already navigated, the history might be in an unexpected state.

Let me look at the Initialize method and see if we need to add layout forcing.

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Excellent debug info! I can see the problems now:

**Problem 1 - Button Height**: 
- Guide button size is `(447, 108)` but doesn't have a LayoutElement
- Our code adds a LayoutElement with `preferredHeight=60` which overrides the 108 height

**Problem 2 - History/Navigation Issue**:
- When you clicked "Survival Manual", `CurrentPageKey` was already `Guides` (not `Home`)!
- Look: `SetPage called: key=Guides, newPage=True, CurrentPageKey=Home` happened BEFORE `Survival Manual clicked`
- This means clicking our button is ALSO triggering the original Guide button's click handler!
- `RemoveAllListeners()` only removes runtime listeners, not **persistent listeners** (serialized in the Unity prefab)

Let me fix both issues:
</details></think>

```
            UnityEngine.Events.UnityAction onClick, TextMeshProUGUI textTemplate)
        {
            // Clone the entire button
            var clone = UnityEngine.Object.Instantiate(template);
            clone.name = name;
            
            // Update the text
            var text = clone.GetComponentInChildren<TextMeshProUGUI>();
            if (text != null)
            {
                text.text = buttonText;
                // Enable word wrapping and center alignment for long text
                text.enableWordWrapping = true;
                text.alignment = TMPro.TextAlignmentOptions.Center;
                text.overflowMode = TMPro.TextOverflowModes.Truncate;
                
                // Keep the same font, size, and style as the template
                if (textTemplate != null)
                {
                    text.font = textTemplate.font;
                    text.fontSize = textTemplate.fontSize;
                    text.fontStyle = textTemplate.fontStyle;
                }
            }
            
            // CRITICAL: Replace the Button's onClick with a NEW UnityEvent to clear persistent listeners
            // RemoveAllListeners() only removes runtime listeners, not serialized/persistent ones!
            var button = clone.GetComponent<Button>();
            if (button != null)
            {
                // Create a completely new onClick event to replace the cloned one
                // This removes any persistent listeners that were serialized from the prefab
                button.onClick = new Button.ButtonClickedEvent();
                button.onClick.AddListener(onClick);
                
                // Clear navigation to prevent button linking issues
                var nav = button.navigation;
                nav.mode = Navigation.Mode.None;
                button.navigation = nav;
            }
            
            // Ensure RectTransform matches template exactly
            var templateRect = template.GetComponent<RectTransform>();
            var cloneRect = clone.GetComponent<RectTransform>();
            if (templateRect != null && cloneRect != null)
            {
                // Copy all RectTransform properties
                cloneRect.anchorMin = templateRect.anchorMin;
                cloneRect.anchorMax = templateRect.anchorMax;
                cloneRect.pivot = templateRect.pivot;
                cloneRect.sizeDelta = templateRect.sizeDelta;
                cloneRect.anchoredPosition = templateRect.anchoredPosition;
            }
            
            // Handle LayoutElement - if template has one, copy it; otherwise use RectTransform size
            var templateLE = template.GetComponent<LayoutElement>();
            var cloneLE = clone.GetComponent<LayoutElement>();
            if (templateLE != null)
            {
                if (cloneLE == null)
                {
                    cloneLE = clone.AddComponent<LayoutElement>();
                }
                cloneLE.minWidth = templateLE.minWidth;
                cloneLE.minHeight = templateLE.minHeight;
                cloneLE.preferredWidth = templateLE.preferredWidth;
                cloneLE.preferredHeight = templateLE.preferredHeight;
                cloneLE.flexibleWidth = templateLE.flexibleWidth;
                cloneLE.flexibleHeight = templateLE.flexibleHeight;
                cloneLE.layoutPriority = templateLE.layoutPriority;
            }
            else if (templateRect != null)
            {
                // No LayoutElement on template - use RectTransform size for preferred dimensions
                if (cloneLE == null)
                {
                    cloneLE = clone.AddComponent<LayoutElement>();
                }
                cloneLE.flexibleWidth = 1;

                cloneLE.preferredHeight = templateRect.sizeDelta.y;
                StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogInfo($"Added LayoutElement with preferredHeight={templateRect.sizeDelta.y} from RectTransform");
            }
            
            return clone;

```
            UnityEngine.Events.UnityAction onClick, TextMeshProUGUI textTemplate)
        {
            // Clone the entire button
            var clone = UnityEngine.Object.Instantiate(template);
            clone.name = name;
            
            // Update the text
            var text = clone.GetComponentInChildren<TextMeshProUGUI>();
            if (text != null)
            {
                text.text = buttonText;
                // Enable word wrapping and center alignment for long text
                text.enableWordWrapping = true;
                text.alignment = TMPro.TextAlignmentOptions.Center;
                text.overflowMode = TMPro.TextOverflowModes.Truncate;
                
                // Keep the same font, size, and style as the template
                if (textTemplate != null)
                {
                    text.font = textTemplate.font;
                    text.fontSize = textTemplate.fontSize;
                    text.fontStyle = textTemplate.fontStyle;
                }
            }
            
            // Replace the click handler - completely replace the Button component's onClick
            var button = clone.GetComponent<Button>();
            if (button != null)
            {
                // Clear ALL persistent and runtime listeners
                button.onClick.RemoveAllListeners();
                button.onClick.AddListener(onClick);
                
                // Clear navigation to prevent button linking issues
                var nav = button.navigation;
                nav.mode = Navigation.Mode.None;
                button.navigation = nav;
            }
            
            // Ensure RectTransform matches template exactly
            var templateRect = template.GetComponent<RectTransform>();
            var cloneRect = clone.GetComponent<RectTransform>();
            if (templateRect != null && cloneRect != null)
            {
                // Copy all RectTransform properties
                cloneRect.anchorMin = templateRect.anchorMin;
                cloneRect.anchorMax = templateRect.anchorMax;
                cloneRect.pivot = templateRect.pivot;
                cloneRect.sizeDelta = templateRect.sizeDelta;
                cloneRect.anchoredPosition = templateRect.anchoredPosition;
            }
            
            // Copy the LayoutElement from template to ensure proper sizing
            var templateLE = template.GetComponent<LayoutElement>();
            var cloneLE = clone.GetComponent<LayoutElement>();
            if (templateLE != null)
            {
                if (cloneLE == null)
                {
                    cloneLE = clone.AddComponent<LayoutElement>();
                }
                cloneLE.minWidth = templateLE.minWidth;
                cloneLE.minHeight = templateLE.minHeight;
                cloneLE.preferredWidth = templateLE.preferredWidth;
                cloneLE.preferredHeight = templateLE.preferredHeight;
                cloneLE.flexibleWidth = templateLE.flexibleWidth;
                cloneLE.flexibleHeight = templateLE.flexibleHeight;
                cloneLE.layoutPriority = templateLE.layoutPriority;
            }
            else
            {
                // No LayoutElement on template - add one with sensible defaults
                if (cloneLE == null)
                {
                    cloneLE = clone.AddComponent<LayoutElement>();
                }
                cloneLE.flexibleWidth = 1;
                cloneLE.preferredHeight = 60; // Match typical button height
            }
            
            return clone;
```

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

Looking at the logs, I can see several issues:

1. **Button sizing**: The Guide button has `size: (447.00, 108.00)` but when I look at `Survival LE: pref=-1x60`, it shows `-1` for width and `60` for height. The `-1` means "no preferred" which is why it's using flexible width. But wait, the template button (Guide) doesn't have a LayoutElement logged... Let me check - I only logged if `guideLE != null`. So the Guide button might not have a LayoutElement at all!

2. **History issue**: Look at the flow:
   - User clicked on "Survival Manual" but `CurrentPageKey` was `Guides` not `Home`!
   - The log shows: `Survival Manual clicked. CurrentPageKey: Guides`
   - Then: `SetPage called: key=SurvivalManual, newPage=True, CurrentPageKey=Guides`
   - Then Back was clicked: `SetPage called: key=Guides, newPage=False, CurrentPageKey=SurvivalManual`
   
   So the issue is clear - when the user clicked "Survival Manual", they were already on the Guides page (CurrentPageKey was Guides), not Home. So the history was correctly added as [Guides, SurvivalManual], and going back correctly went to Guides.

   But wait - the user was on the HOME page visually when they clicked the button, but `CurrentPageKey` was `Guides`. This means `CurrentPageKey` is out of sync with the actual displayed page!

   Looking earlier in the log:
   ```
   SetPage called: key=Home, newPage=True, CurrentPageKey=Home
   SetPage called: key=Guides, newPage=True, CurrentPageKey=Home
   Survival Manual clicked. CurrentPageKey: Guides
   ```
   
   So SetPage was called with `key=Guides` right before the Survival Manual click. This is strange - the user was on Home, clicked Survival Manual button, but Guides got called first?
   
   The cloned buttons might be triggering their original listeners even after calling `RemoveAllListeners()`. Looking at the sequence, SetPage for Guides was invoked before the Survival Manual click was registered, suggesting either the click event is propagating to the template button or there's a Unity event system issue with the cloned buttons.

   I disabled Navigation mode but something else is still calling SetPage. The buttons aren't layered over the originals since I reordered the container hierarchy. The real problem might be that persistent listeners attached in the inspector aren't being cleared—`RemoveAllListeners()` only removes runtime listeners, not the ones configured in the editor.

   For the button sizing, the Guide button is 108 pixels tall with no LayoutElement, so its size comes from the RectTransform. When I add a LayoutElement with preferredHeight of 60, that overrides the original dimensions, which is causing the mismatch.

   I need to address two problems: first, clear the persistent listeners by creating a fresh onClick event instead of cloning the old one, and second, either skip adding a LayoutElement entirely or match its preferredHeight to the template's RectTransform height.

The buttons are still squished. Let me think about what's happening:

1. The log showed: `Guide button size: (447.00, 108.00), Survival button size: (447.00, 108.00)`
2. But also: `Survival LE: pref=-1x60, flex=1x-1`

So the RectTransform is correctly cloned at 108 height, but the LayoutElement has `preferredHeight=60`. This means even with my fix, the LayoutElement is being added with the wrong height.

Wait, looking at the log more carefully - the Guide button doesn't have a LayoutElement (there was no "Guide LE:" log line), so our code is hitting the `else if (templateRect != null)` branch and should be using `templateRect.sizeDelta.y` which is 108.

But the log shows `Survival LE: pref=-1x60` - that's `preferredHeight=60`, not 108. 

Hmm, but wait - that log was from BEFORE my latest fix. The latest fix changed the preferredHeight from 60 to `templateRect.sizeDelta.y`.

But actually, looking at this more carefully - the issue might be with the container's layout settings overriding the button sizes. The HorizontalLayoutGroup on our container might be controlling the height.

Let me check the container setup. The problem could be:
1. `childControlHeight = true` on the HorizontalLayoutGroup - this makes the layout control child heights
2. The container's own LayoutElement with `preferredHeight = 50` or similar

Let me look at the container setup code and the log output more carefully. The container is copying from guideButtonContainer:

```csharp
var templateLayout = guideButtonContainer.GetComponent<HorizontalLayoutGroup>();
if (templateLayout != null)
{
    var customLayout = _customButtonContainer.AddComponent<HorizontalLayoutGroup>();
    customLayout.spacing = templateLayout.spacing;
    customLayout.padding = templateLayout.padding;
    customLayout.childAlignment = templateLayout.childAlignment;
    customLayout.childControlWidth = templateLayout.childControlWidth;
    customLayout.childControlHeight = templateLayout.childControlHeight;  // <-- This might be forcing height!
    customLayout.childForceExpandWidth = templateLayout.childForceExpandWidth;
    customLayout.childForceExpandHeight = templateLayout.childForceExpandHeight;
}
```

And then the container's own LayoutElement is being copied from the template. If `childControlHeight` is true on the HorizontalLayoutGroup, it will override the button's preferred heights and force them to match the container's height. That's likely the culprit - the container itself might have a LayoutElement with a smaller preferred height that's constraining everything inside it. The issue is that I'm setting a fixed container height of 50 pixels when there's no template LayoutElement, which crushes the buttons inside. Instead, I should either inherit the template's height or let the container size itself based on its children. I'll remove the hardcoded constraint and add logging to track the layout behavior.

+### SolarIrradiance Calculation (From LocalSolarIrradiance)
+
+SolarIrradiance is calculated as follows:
+
+$$
+\text{SolarIrradiance} = \begin{cases}
+0 & \text{if } \neg\mathrm{HasLight} \\
+\mathrm{OrbitalSimulation.SolarIrradiance} \times \mathrm{WeatherEvent.SolarRatio} & \text{otherwise}
+\end{cases}
+$$
*** End Patch

```
I need you to research the codebase to understand how to implement a new guide system for Stationpedia. DO NOT write a plan, only gather comprehensive context and return structured findings.

## Research Goals:

### 1. Understand How Vanilla Guides Work
- Look in the mod folder at `c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaAscended\mod\` for:
  - How guides are currently registered/displayed
  - How SetPageGuides populates the guide buttons
  - The GuidesPages list structure
  - How guide content is rendered

### 2. Understand the Survival Manual Format
- Find and analyze `SurvivalManualLoader.cs` 
- Understand how markdown is parsed into OperationalDetails
- How collapsible sections work with nesting
- How Table of Contents is generated
- How ParsedPart and ParsedSection structures work

### 3. Understand Device Linking Mechanism
- Find how {THING:PrefabName} links work in Stationpedia
- How to make text clickable to navigate to device pages
- Look for TextMeshPro link formatting

### 4. Understand Nested Categories
- Find if there's already a category/nesting system for guides
- How collapsible categories work in the guide list UI

### 5. Look at the Daylight Sensor Guide
- Read `c:\Users\marka\Downloads\daylight-sensor-guide.md`
- Identify all sections that need to be converted
- Note the structure and which device names appear

## Device Keys Found (for reference):
- Daylight Sensor: ThingStructureDaylightSensor
- Solar Panel: ThingStructureSolarPanel
- Solar Panel (Dual): ThingStructureSolarPanelDual
- Logic Writer: ThingStructureLogicWriter
- Batch Writer: ThingStructureLogicBatchWriter
- Logic Memory: ThingStructureLogicMemory
- Battery (Small): ThingStructureBatterySmall
- Battery (Medium): ThingStructureBatteryMedium
- Programmable Chip: ThingMotherboardProgrammableChip
- Air Conditioner: ThingStructureAirConditioner
- Tablet: ThingItemAdvancedTablet
- Circuitboard (Air Control): ThingCircuitboardAirControl

## Return Format:
Provide structured findings organized by:
1. Current Guide System Architecture (how it works now)
2. Survival Manual Loader Analysis (parsing, sections, TOC)
3. Device Linking Mechanism (how to create clickable links)
4. UI Nesting Patterns (how to create collapsible categories)
5. Daylight Sensor Guide Analysis (content structure, device references)
6. Key Files to Modify/Create
7. Important Code Patterns to Follow

Work autonomously and gather all context needed. Don't ask questions, just research thoroughly.
```


</details></tool-use>

- description: `Phase 1: GuideLoader base system`
- prompt:

```
## Phase 1: Create GuideLoader Base System

**Objective:** Create a reusable GuideLoader class for parsing and registering custom guides from markdown files.

**Context:**
- Working in `c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaAscended\mod\`
- This mod extends Stationpedia in the game Stationeers using BepInEx/Harmony
- There's an existing `SurvivalManualLoader.cs` that parses markdown - use it as a pattern reference
- The mod already uses `DeviceDescription` and `OperationalDetail` classes for content

**User Requirements:**
- Tables should use plain indented rows without borders (look at how game renders logic types)
- MIPS code blocks should have syntax highlighting (color-coded keywords)
- Device names should convert to `{THING:PrefabKey}` links for clickable navigation

**Files to Create:**
1. `c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaAscended\mod\GuideLoader.cs`

**Key Functions to Implement:**
1. `GuideLoader.LoadGuide(string filename, string pageKey, string displayTitle)` - Load and parse a markdown guide file
2. `GuideLoader.ParseMarkdownToGuide(string content)` - Convert markdown to List<OperationalDetail>
3. `GuideLoader.ConvertDeviceReferences(string text)` - Replace device names with `{THING:PrefabKey}` links
4. `GuideLoader.FormatMipsCode(string code)` - Add syntax highlighting to MIPS code blocks

**Device Name Mapping (use these exact mappings):**
```
"Daylight Sensor" → "{THING:ThingStructureDaylightSensor}"
"Solar Panel" → "{THING:ThingStructureSolarPanel}"
"Solar Panel (Dual)" or "StructureSolarPanelDual" → "{THING:ThingStructureSolarPanelDual}"
"Logic Writer" → "{THING:ThingStructureLogicWriter}"
"Batch Writer" → "{THING:ThingStructureLogicBatchWriter}"
"Logic Memory" → "{THING:ThingStructureLogicMemory}"
"Battery" (generic) → "{THING:ThingStructureBatterySmall}"
"Programmable Chip" or "IC10" → "{THING:ThingMotherboardProgrammableChip}"
"Air Conditioner" → "{THING:ThingStructureAirConditioner}"
"Tablet" → "{THING:ThingItemAdvancedTablet}"
```

**MIPS Syntax Highlighting Colors:**
- Keywords (alias, define, yield, j, jal, jr): `<color=#569CD6>keyword</color>` (blue)
- Registers (r0-r15, sp, ra, db, d0-d5): `<color=#9CDCFE>register</color>` (light blue)
- Labels (word followed by colon): `<color=#DCDCAA>label:</color>` (yellow)
- Comments (# to end of line): `<color=#6A9955># comment</color>` (green)
- Numbers: `<color=#B5CEA8>123</color>` (light green)
- Instructions (l, s, add, sub, mul, div, mod, beq, bne, bgt, blt, bgtz, etc.): `<color=#C586C0>instruction</color>` (purple)

**Markdown Parsing Rules:**
- `# Title` → Guide title (root OperationalDetail)
- `## Section` → Major section (collapsible, child of root)
- `### Subsection` → Subsection (collapsible, child of section)
- `**bold**` → `<b>bold</b>`
- `` `code` `` → `<color=#88CCFF>code</color>`
- Code blocks (```mips ... ```) → formatted with syntax highlighting
- Tables (| col | col |) → plain indented text rows
- `{THING:Key}` format is preserved as-is (native format)

**Reference Files to Read:**
- `c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaAscended\mod\SurvivalManualLoader.cs` - Pattern for markdown parsing
- `c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaAscended\mod\DeviceDescription.cs` - Data structures

**Instructions:**
1. Read SurvivalManualLoader.cs to understand the existing pattern
2. Read DeviceDescription.cs to understand the data structures
3. Create GuideLoader.cs with all the required functions
4. Follow TDD principles - but since this is a Unity mod without easy test framework, focus on defensive coding with null checks
5. Work autonomously - implement the complete solution
6. Do NOT proceed to other phases - only implement Phase 1

**Return:** Summary of what was implemented, files created/modified, and any issues encountered.
```


</details></tool-use>

- description: `Phase 2: Markdown table conversion`
- prompt:

```
## Phase 2: Implement Markdown Table Conversion

**Objective:** Add markdown table parsing to GuideLoader that converts tables to plain indented text (similar to how the game renders logic types on device pages).

**Context:**
- Working in `c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaAscended\mod\`
- GuideLoader.cs was just created in Phase 1 at `src/Data/GuideLoader.cs`
- Tables should NOT use ASCII borders - just plain indented rows

**User Requirement:**
> Tables should use plain indented rows without borders. Take a look at how the game renders logic types for a table style on device pages.

**Example Input:**
```markdown
| Mode | Value | SolarAngle Output |
|------|-------|-------------------|
| Default | 0 | Sensor-to-sun angle |
| Horizontal | 1 | Sun azimuth |
| Vertical | 2 | Sun elevation |
```

**Example Output (plain indented style):**
```
<b>Mode</b>: Default
    Value: 0
    SolarAngle Output: Sensor-to-sun angle

<b>Mode</b>: Horizontal
    Value: 1
    SolarAngle Output: Sun azimuth

<b>Mode</b>: Vertical
    Value: 2
    SolarAngle Output: Sun elevation
```

The format should be:
- First column value becomes the header (bold)
- Remaining columns become indented key-value pairs beneath it
- Blank line between each row for readability

**Files to Modify:**
1. `c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaAscended\mod\src\Data\GuideLoader.cs`

**Functions to Add/Modify:**
1. `ParseMarkdownTable(List<string> tableLines)` - New function to convert table lines to formatted string
2. `ParseMarkdownToGuide()` - Modify to detect table blocks and call ParseMarkdownTable
3. `IsTableLine(string line)` - Helper to detect if a line is part of a table (starts with |)
4. `IsSeparatorLine(string line)` - Helper to detect table separator (|---|---|)

**Table Parsing Logic:**
1. Detect table start: line starts with `|`
2. Extract header row (first `|` line)
3. Skip separator row (`|---|---|`)
4. Parse data rows
5. Format as indented key-value pairs using first column as header

**Edge Cases to Handle:**
- Tables with 2 columns (simple key-value)
- Tables with 3+ columns
- Tables inside code blocks should NOT be converted
- Empty cells
- Cells with inline formatting (`**bold**`, `code`)

**Instructions:**
1. Read the current GuideLoader.cs to understand its structure
2. Add table parsing functions
3. Integrate table detection into the main parsing flow
4. Build and verify no errors
5. Work autonomously - implement the complete solution
6. Do NOT proceed to other phases - only implement Phase 2

**Return:** Summary of what was modified, functions added, and build status.
```


</details></tool-use>

```
## Phase 3: Add Daylight Sensor Guide

**Objective:** Copy the Daylight Sensor guide markdown file to the mod, register it as a custom guide, and add SetPage handling so it can be displayed.

**Context:**
- Working in `c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaAscended\mod\`
- GuideLoader.cs is ready at `src/Data/GuideLoader.cs` with all parsing functions
- The guide source is at `c:\Users\marka\Downloads\daylight-sensor-guide.md`
- Existing guides like "SurvivalManual" are handled in Patches.cs

**Files to Create:**
1. `c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaAscended\mod\guides\daylight-sensor-guide.md` - Copy of the guide

**Files to Modify:**
1. `c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaAscended\mod\Plugin.cs` - Register the guide
2. `c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaAscended\mod\Patches.cs` - Add SetPage handler for "DaylightSensorGuide"
3. `c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaAscended\mod\StationpediaAscended.csproj` - Include the guide file as embedded resource or content

**Implementation Steps:**

### Step 1: Create guides folder and copy markdown
- Create `guides/` folder in the mod directory
- Copy `daylight-sensor-guide.md` from Downloads to `guides/`

### Step 2: Update csproj to include the guide file
Add to StationpediaAscended.csproj:
```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Content Include="guides\**\*.md">
    <CopyToOutputDirectory>PreserveNewest</CopyToOutputDirectory>
  </Content>
</ItemGroup>
```

### Step 3: Add guide registration to Plugin.cs
Look at how other pages are registered. In the Awake() method or similar initialization, add:
```csharp
// Load and register Daylight Sensor Guide
var daylightGuide = GuideLoader.LoadGuide("daylight-sensor-guide.md", "DaylightSensorGuide", "Daylight Sensor Guide");
if (daylightGuide != null)
{
    CustomGuides["DaylightSensorGuide"] = daylightGuide;
}
```

You may need to create a `CustomGuides` dictionary in Plugin.cs or another appropriate location.

### Step 4: Add SetPage handling in Patches.cs
Look at how "SurvivalManual" is handled in `Stationpedia_SetPage_Prefix`. Add similar handling for "DaylightSensorGuide":

```csharp
if (key == "DaylightSensorGuide")
{
    // Similar to SurvivalManual handling
    // Use existing RenderOperationalDetails pattern
    return false; // Skip vanilla handling
}
```

**Key Patterns to Follow:**
- Look at `SurvivalManualLoader` for how it registers content
- Look at `Stationpedia_SetPage_Prefix` in Patches.cs for the SetPage interception pattern
- Look at how `PopulateSurvivalManualContents` renders OperationalDetails

**Reference Files to Read:**
- `c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaAscended\mod\Plugin.cs`
- `c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaAscended\mod\Patches.cs`
- `c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaAscended\mod\SurvivalManualLoader.cs`
- `c:\Users\marka\Downloads\daylight-sensor-guide.md` (the source guide)

**Instructions:**
1. Read the existing Plugin.cs and Patches.cs to understand the patterns
2. Read the daylight-sensor-guide.md to understand its structure
3. Create the guides folder and copy the markdown file
4. Update csproj to include the guide
5. Add registration in Plugin.cs
6. Add SetPage handling in Patches.cs
7. Build and verify no errors
8. Work autonomously - implement the complete solution
9. Do NOT proceed to other phases - only implement Phase 3

**Return:** Summary of files created/modified, registration approach used, and build status.
```


</details></tool-use>

```
## Phase 4: Create Guide Button and Home Page Integration

**Objective:** Add a blue "Daylight Sensor Guide" button to the Guides page that opens the new guide with proper navigation. The button should appear ABOVE the vanilla guides category.

**Context:**
- Working in `c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaAscended\mod\`
- The DaylightSensorGuide is now registered and can be displayed via SetPage
- HomePageLayoutManager.cs handles custom button creation on the home page
- Need to add a button specifically on the GUIDES PAGE (SetPageGuides), not the home page

**User Requirement:**
> Custom guide button placement: Above the "Vanilla Guides" category

**Files to Modify:**

// Pattern from HomePageLayoutManager

button.onClick.AddListener(() => Stationpedia.SetPage("DaylightSensorGuide"));
```

```
## Phase 5: Nest Vanilla Guides Under Category

**Objective:** Organize existing vanilla guides under a collapsible "Vanilla Guides" category in the Guides page. The category should start COLLAPSED by default.

**Context:**
- Working in `c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaAscended\mod\`
- Phase 4 added a custom guide button at the TOP of the guides list
- Now we need to wrap all the vanilla guide buttons in a collapsible category
- The "Vanilla Guides" category should appear BELOW our custom guide button

**User Requirements:**
> Vanilla Guides category should start collapsed when the page is opened

**Current Guides Page Structure (after Phase 4):**
```
[Daylight Sensor Guide] ← Custom button (blue)
[Guide 1] ← Vanilla
[Guide 2] ← Vanilla
[Guide 3] ← Vanilla
...
```

**Target Guides Page Structure:**
```
[Daylight Sensor Guide] ← Custom button (blue)
[▼ Vanilla Guides] ← Collapsible category (COLLAPSED by default)
    [Guide 1]
    [Guide 2]
    [Guide 3]
    ...
```

**Files to Modify:**
1. `c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaAscended\mod\src\Harmony\Patches.cs` - Modify SetPageGuides_Postfix

**Implementation Approach:**

### Modify the SetPageGuides Postfix to wrap vanilla guides

After the custom button is added, we need to:

1. Get all SPDAListItem children in the guide container (these are vanilla guides)
2. Create a new StationpediaCategory (collapsible container)
3. Move all vanilla guide items INTO the category's Contents
4. Set the category title to "Vanilla Guides"
5. Set the category to COLLAPSED state by default
6. Add the category to the container

**Key Pattern - Use StationpediaCategory prefab:**

Look at how `SurvivalManualLoader` or `PopulateGameMechanicsContents` creates collapsible sections. They use:
- `Stationpedia.instance._stationpediaCategoryPrefab` - The collapsible category prefab
- `category.SetTitle()` or set `category.Title.text`
- `category.Contents` - The child container to parent items into
- `category.IsExpanded` or `category.Toggle()` - Expand/collapse state

**Example Pattern from SurvivalManualLoader:**
```csharp
var category = UnityEngine.Object.Instantiate(
    Stationpedia.instance._stationpediaCategoryPrefab,
    parentContainer
);
category.Title.text = "Vanilla Guides";
category.Contents.gameObject.SetActive(false); // Start collapsed

// Move vanilla items into category
foreach (var item in vanillaItems)
{
    item.transform.SetParent(category.Contents, false);
}
```

**Steps in SetPageGuides_Postfix (after AddCustomGuideButton):**

1. Get reference to `_loreGuideItemList` container
2. Collect all vanilla SPDAListItem children (skip our custom button)
3. Instantiate StationpediaCategory prefab
4. Set title to "Vanilla Guides"
5. Move all vanilla items into category.Contents
6. Insert category as second child (after our custom button)
7. Collapse the category by default

**Reference Files to Read:**
- `c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaAscended\mod\src\Harmony\Patches.cs` - Current implementation
- `c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaAscended\mod\SurvivalManualLoader.cs` - StationpediaCategory usage
- Search Assembly-CSharp for StationpediaCategory to understand its API

**Important:**
- Make sure the custom "Daylight Sensor Guide" button is NOT moved into the category
- The order should be: Custom button first, then Vanilla Guides category
- Category must start COLLAPSED (user requirement)

**Instructions:**
1. Read Patches.cs to see current SetPageGuides_Postfix
2. Read SurvivalManualLoader.cs for StationpediaCategory patterns
3. Search for StationpediaCategory in Assembly-CSharp for API details
4. Modify SetPageGuides_Postfix to wrap vanilla guides in collapsible category
5. Ensure category starts collapsed
6. Build and verify no errors
7. Work autonomously - implement the complete solution
8. This is the FINAL phase - complete it fully

**Return:** Summary of modifications made, the nesting approach used, and build status.
```

_**User (2026-01-16 06:07:54Z)**_

Ok we are going to take a detour on a new project. What you just did diol to mockup thsee things and figure out what format I want them to be in so please use your conudc tor instructions but dont wait to ask me questions or a git commit. Please make a enw folder in our project for this:

You are a senior full-stack engineer. Build a desktop-like, fast GUI editor for the Stationeers mod “Stationpedia Ascended”. This tool is a “word-processor style” editor for three content types we add to the Stationpedia system:
1) Game Guides
2) Device Mechanics
3) Operational Details

PRIMARY GOALS
- Very fast browsing/searching/sorting through all Stationpedia pages (should feel instant).
- Beautiful, smooth HTML-based UI (easy to tweak front-end).
- Direct editing of the underlying JSON files in the mod (no manual hand-editing).
- Live preview approximating the in-game Stationpedia look and structure (nested dropdowns/buttons, etc.).
- Rich text editing similar to TextMesh Pro: bold, italics, links to other Stationpedia pages.
- Ability to edit all three sections above in one unified tool.

ASSUMPTIONS / CONTEXT
- The mod contains Stationpedia content JSON files (e.g., “descriptions.json” and related files/folders).
- The mod also contains PNG assets (backgrounds, icons, etc.) that can be used in preview to approximate the in-game Stationpedia UI.
- We don’t need to recreate Unity; we just want a faithful approximation for authoring and validation.

TECH STACK REQUIREMENTS (pick one and implement fully)
Option A (preferred): Electron + React + TypeScript + Vite + Node filesystem access.
Option B: Tauri + React + TypeScript + Rust backend for filesystem.
Choose Option A unless there is a compelling reason to choose B.

CORE FEATURES
1) Project/Workspace
- User selects a root folder for the mod content.
- App indexes all relevant Stationpedia JSON content and assets.
- Remembers last workspace opened.

2) Instant navigation
- Left sidebar: searchable list/tree of pages (devices/guides/mechanics/operational details).
- Search should be fast (use in-memory index like Fuse.js or your own).
- Sorting/filtering (by category, device name, tags, “has missing link”, “unreviewed”, etc.).

3) Editor (word processor feel)
- Central editor pane with rich text editing.
- MUST support: headings (H1/H2/H3), bullet lists, numbered lists, bold, italic, underline (if available), inline code (optional), and especially:
  - Internal links to other Stationpedia pages via a link picker UI (don’t force the user to type IDs manually).
- Provide a “source view” toggle:
  - Visual editor (default)
  - Source view showing the underlying markup/representation (TextMeshPro-ish / custom / markdown-like), editable.

4) Live Preview (Stationpedia approximation)
- Right pane: preview of how the page will look in-game.
- Use mod PNG backgrounds/icons to mimic Stationpedia styling.
- Must render nested dropdowns / button groups / collapsibles similar to the in-game layout (approximate is OK, but structure must be accurate).
- Preview updates live as you type (debounced).

5) Page structure + sections
- Each page can have multiple sections/subsections (dropdown/collapsible groups).
- Support adding/removing/reordering sections via drag-and-drop.
- Support adding “Operational Details” blocks, “Device Mechanics” blocks, and “Guide Steps” blocks.
- Each block type can have its own fields (title, body, prerequisites, warnings, etc.) if present in JSON schema.

6) Link integrity + validation
- Internal links should be validated:
  - Broken link detection.
  - Link picker should show valid targets.
- Validation panel: missing required fields, invalid JSON schema, broken assets, etc.

7) File IO + Safety
- Read/write JSON safely.
- Autosave option + manual save.
- Keep backups / change history:
  - At minimum: write a timestamped backup copy before overwriting a file OR maintain a simple local history folder.
- Confirm before destructive actions.

8) Performance
- Index all content on load into memory for speed.
- Use virtualization in long lists (e.g., react-window).
- Debounce preview rendering.
- No laggy re-renders.

DATA MODEL / SCHEMA
- Implement a flexible schema layer:
  - Parse JSON into typed models.
  - If exact schema is unknown, implement a “best effort” mapping and allow unknown fields to round-trip unchanged.
  - Preserve ordering and formatting as much as reasonable.
- The tool must not destroy fields it doesn’t understand.

RICH TEXT STORAGE FORMAT
We need a storage format that round-trips and can export to whatever the mod needs.
Implement this approach:
- Internally store rich text as a structured JSON AST (like ProseMirror or TipTap’s JSON).
- Provide exporters/importers:
  - Export to the mod’s expected markup (TextMeshPro-like tags if needed).
  - Import existing mod markup into the editor.
- If the mod currently uses TextMeshPro tags (e.g., <b>, <i>, <link=...>), support those.
- If unknown, implement adapters with clear TODO markers and examples.

DELIVERABLES
A) Working app codebase with:
- Electron + React + TS + Vite
- File system access (open folder, read/write files)
- UI: sidebar list, editor, preview, validation panel
B) Clear README with:
- Setup instructions
- Build/run instructions
- Where to configure the folder paths
- How the JSON schema is handled
C) Example fixtures
- Provide a sample “descriptions.json” structure (mock) and sample assets folder to test, if real files are not available.
D) Architecture notes
- Explain indexing strategy
- Explain rich text conversion strategy
- Explain how unknown JSON fields are preserved

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS (do these, don’t just describe them)
- Create the repository structure.
- Create the UI layout with panes (sidebar/editor/preview/validation).
- Implement folder open + indexing.
- Implement fast search.
- Implement the rich text editor (TipTap preferred).
- Implement link picker modal (search targets + insert link).
- Implement preview renderer.
- Implement save + backup history.
- Implement validation (basic first: broken links, missing titles, JSON parse errors).
- Provide a minimal but functional version first, then list incremental improvements.

OUTPUT FORMAT
- Provide:
  1) A short overview of the architecture.
  2) A step-by-step plan.
  3) The full code for the initial working version (multiple files).
  4) Instructions to run it locally.
  5) A TODO list for next enhancements.

IMPORTANT UX NOTES
- The editor must feel like a “word processor,” not a raw JSON editor.
- But source view must exist for power users.
- Avoid slow operations; everything should feel instant.

If anything is ambiguous, make reasonable engineering assumptions and document them in README instead of asking questions.

import { defineConfig } from 'vite'
import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'
import electron from 'vite-plugin-electron'
import renderer from 'vite-plugin-electron-renderer'
import path from 'path'

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [
    react(),
    electron([
      {
        entry: 'src/main/index.ts',
        vite: {
          build: {
            outDir: 'dist-electron/main',
            rollupOptions: {
              external: ['electron'],
            },
          },
        },
      },
      {
        entry: 'src/preload/index.ts',
        vite: {
          build: {
            outDir: 'dist-electron/preload',
            rollupOptions: {
              external: ['electron'],
            },
          },
        },
        onstart(options) {
          options.reload()
        },
      },
    ]),
    renderer(),
  ],
  resolve: {
    alias: {
      '@': path.resolve(__dirname, './src'),
      '@components': path.resolve(__dirname, './src/renderer/components'),
      '@services': path.resolve(__dirname, './src/renderer/services'),
      '@types': path.resolve(__dirname, './src/renderer/types'),
      '@styles': path.resolve(__dirname, './src/renderer/styles'),
      '@store': path.resolve(__dirname, './src/renderer/store'),
    },
  },
  root: path.resolve(__dirname, './src/renderer'),
  base: './',
  server: {
    port: 5173,
  },
  build: {
    outDir: path.resolve(__dirname, './dist'),
    emptyOutDir: true,
    rollupOptions: {
      input: path.resolve(__dirname, './src/renderer/index.html'),
    },
  },
})

<summary>Tool use: **replace_string_in_file**</summary>

**Input:**

- newString:

```
/**
 * Electron Main Process
 */
import { app, BrowserWindow, ipcMain, dialog } from 'electron';
import * as fs from 'fs';
import * as path from 'path';

let mainWindow: BrowserWindow | null = null;

function createWindow() {
  mainWindow = new BrowserWindow({
    width: 1600,
    height: 1000,
    minWidth: 1200,
    minHeight: 700,
    webPreferences: {
      preload: path.join(__dirname, '../preload/index.js'),
      contextIsolation: true,
      nodeIntegration: false,
    },
    backgroundColor: '#1a1a1a',
    show: false,
  });

  mainWindow.once('ready-to-show', () => {
    mainWindow?.show();
  });

  // Load the app
  if (process.env.VITE_DEV_SERVER_URL) {
    mainWindow.loadURL(process.env.VITE_DEV_SERVER_URL);
    mainWindow.webContents.openDevTools();
  } else {
    mainWindow.loadFile(path.join(__dirname, '../../dist/index.html'));
  }

  mainWindow.on('closed', () => {
    mainWindow = null;
  });
}

app.whenReady().then(createWindow);

app.on('window-all-closed', () => {
  if (process.platform !== 'darwin') {
    app.quit();
  }
});

app.on('activate', () => {
  if (mainWindow === null) {
    createWindow();
  }
});

// IPC Handlers

// Open folder dialog
ipcMain.handle('dialog:openFolder', async () => {
  const result = await dialog.showOpenDialog(mainWindow!, {
    properties: ['openDirectory'],
    title: 'Select Mod Folder',
  });
  if (result.canceled || result.filePaths.length === 0) {
    return null;
  }
  return result.filePaths[0];
});

// Read file
ipcMain.handle('fs:readFile', async (_, filePath: string) => {
  try {
    const content = fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8');
    return { success: true, data: content };
  } catch (error: any) {
    return { success: false, error: error.message };
  }
});

// Write file with backup
ipcMain.handle('fs:writeFile', async (_, filePath: string, content: string, createBackup: boolean) => {
  try {
    // Create backup if requested
    if (createBackup && fs.existsSync(filePath)) {
      const backupDir = path.join(path.dirname(filePath), '.backups');
      if (!fs.existsSync(backupDir)) {
        fs.mkdirSync(backupDir, { recursive: true });
      }
      const timestamp = new Date().toISOString().replace(/[:.]/g, '-');
      const backupPath = path.join(backupDir, `${path.basename(filePath)}.${timestamp}.bak`);
      fs.copyFileSync(filePath, backupPath);
    }

    fs.writeFileSync(filePath, content, 'utf-8');
    return { success: true };
  } catch (error: any) {
    return { success: false, error: error.message };
  }
});

// List files in directory
ipcMain.handle('fs:listFiles', async (_, directory: string, extensions: string[]) => {
  try {
    const files: string[] = [];
    
    function scanDir(dir: string) {
      const entries = fs.readdirSync(dir, { withFileTypes: true });
      for (const entry of entries) {
        const fullPath = path.join(dir, entry.name);
        if (entry.isDirectory() && !entry.name.startsWith('.')) {
          scanDir(fullPath);
        } else if (entry.isFile()) {
          const ext = path.extname(entry.name).toLowerCase();
          if (extensions.length === 0 || extensions.includes(ext)) {
            files.push(fullPath);
          }
        }
      }
    }
    
    if (fs.existsSync(directory)) {
      scanDir(directory);
    }
    
    return { success: true, data: files };
  } catch (error: any) {
    return { success: false, error: error.message };
  }
});

// Check if path exists
ipcMain.handle('fs:exists', async (_, filePath: string) => {
  return fs.existsSync(filePath);
});

// Get file info
ipcMain.handle('fs:stat', async (_, filePath: string) => {
  try {
    const stats = fs.statSync(filePath);
    return {
      success: true,
      data: {
        isFile: stats.isFile(),
        isDirectory: stats.isDirectory(),
        size: stats.size,
        modified: stats.mtime.toISOString(),
      },
    };
  } catch (error: any) {
    return { success: false, error: error.message };
  }
});

// Store and retrieve last workspace
const userDataPath = app.getPath('userData');
const settingsPath = path.join(userDataPath, 'settings.json');

ipcMain.handle('settings:get', async () => {
  try {
    if (fs.existsSync(settingsPath)) {
      const content = fs.readFileSync(settingsPath, 'utf-8');
      return JSON.parse(content);
    }
    return {};
  } catch {
    return {};
  }
});

ipcMain.handle('settings:set', async (_, settings: object) => {
  try {
    fs.writeFileSync(settingsPath, JSON.stringify(settings, null, 2), 'utf-8');
    return { success: true };
  } catch (error: any) {
    return { success: false, error: error.message };
  }
});
```

<summary>Tool use: **replace_string_in_file**</summary>

**Input:**

- newString:

```
/**
 * Preload Script - Exposes safe IPC methods to renderer
 */
import { contextBridge, ipcRenderer } from 'electron';

// Expose file system API
contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld('electronAPI', {
  // Dialog
  openFolder: () => ipcRenderer.invoke('dialog:openFolder'),

  // File system
  readFile: (filePath: string) => ipcRenderer.invoke('fs:readFile', filePath),
  writeFile: (filePath: string, content: string, createBackup: boolean = true) =>
    ipcRenderer.invoke('fs:writeFile', filePath, content, createBackup),
  listFiles: (directory: string, extensions: string[] = []) =>
    ipcRenderer.invoke('fs:listFiles', directory, extensions),
  exists: (filePath: string) => ipcRenderer.invoke('fs:exists', filePath),
  stat: (filePath: string) => ipcRenderer.invoke('fs:stat', filePath),

  // Settings
  getSettings: () => ipcRenderer.invoke('settings:get'),
  setSettings: (settings: object) => ipcRenderer.invoke('settings:set', settings),
});

// Type declarations for TypeScript
export interface ElectronAPI {
  openFolder: () => Promise<string | null>;
  readFile: (filePath: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean; data?: string; error?: string }>;
  writeFile: (filePath: string, content: string, createBackup?: boolean) => Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>;
  listFiles: (directory: string, extensions?: string[]) => Promise<{ success: boolean; data?: string[]; error?: string }>;
  exists: (filePath: string) => Promise<boolean>;
  stat: (filePath: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean; data?: { isFile: boolean; isDirectory: boolean; size: number; modified: string }; error?: string }>;
  getSettings: () => Promise<Record<string, any>>;
  setSettings: (settings: object) => Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>;
}

declare global {
  interface Window {
    electronAPI: ElectronAPI;
  }
}
```

```
/**
 * Zustand Store for Application State
 */
import { create } from 'zustand';
import type { DeviceDescription, ValidationError, OperationalDetail } from '@types/stationpedia';

export interface WorkspaceState {
  // Workspace
  workspacePath: string | null;
  isLoading: boolean;
  lastError: string | null;

  // Content
  devices: DeviceDescription[];
  guides: DeviceDescription[];
  mechanics: DeviceDescription[];
  allContent: DeviceDescription[];

  // Selection
  selectedItem: DeviceDescription | null;
  selectedType: 'device' | 'guide' | 'mechanic' | null;

  // Editor
  isDirty: boolean;
  editorContent: string;
  isSourceView: boolean;

  // Validation
  validationErrors: ValidationError[];

  // Actions
  setWorkspacePath: (path: string | null) => void;
  setLoading: (loading: boolean) => void;
  setError: (error: string | null) => void;
  setDevices: (devices: DeviceDescription[]) => void;
  setGuides: (guides: DeviceDescription[]) => void;
  setMechanics: (mechanics: DeviceDescription[]) => void;
  setAllContent: (content: DeviceDescription[]) => void;
  selectItem: (item: DeviceDescription | null, type?: 'device' | 'guide' | 'mechanic') => void;
  setDirty: (dirty: boolean) => void;
  setEditorContent: (content: string) => void;
  toggleSourceView: () => void;
  setValidationErrors: (errors: ValidationError[]) => void;
  updateSelectedItem: (updates: Partial<DeviceDescription>) => void;
  updateOperationalDetail: (index: number, detail: OperationalDetail) => void;
  addOperationalDetail: (detail: OperationalDetail) => void;
  removeOperationalDetail: (index: number) => void;
  reorderOperationalDetails: (fromIndex: number, toIndex: number) => void;
  reset: () => void;
}

const initialState = {
  workspacePath: null,
  isLoading: false,
  lastError: null,
  devices: [],
  guides: [],
  mechanics: [],
  allContent: [],
  selectedItem: null,
  selectedType: null,
  isDirty: false,
  editorContent: '',
  isSourceView: false,
  validationErrors: [],
};

export const useStore = create<WorkspaceState>((set, get) => ({
  ...initialState,

  setWorkspacePath: (path) => set({ workspacePath: path }),
  setLoading: (loading) => set({ isLoading: loading }),
  setError: (error) => set({ lastError: error }),
  
  setDevices: (devices) => set({ devices }),
  setGuides: (guides) => set({ guides }),
  setMechanics: (mechanics) => set({ mechanics }),
  setAllContent: (content) => set({ allContent: content }),

  selectItem: (item, type = 'device') => set({
    selectedItem: item,
    selectedType: type,
    editorContent: item?.pageDescription || '',
    isDirty: false,
  }),

  setDirty: (dirty) => set({ isDirty: dirty }),
  setEditorContent: (content) => set({ editorContent: content, isDirty: true }),
  toggleSourceView: () => set((state) => ({ isSourceView: !state.isSourceView })),
  setValidationErrors: (errors) => set({ validationErrors: errors }),

  updateSelectedItem: (updates) => {
    const { selectedItem, devices, guides, mechanics } = get();
    if (!selectedItem) return;

    const updated = { ...selectedItem, ...updates };
    set({ selectedItem: updated, isDirty: true });

    // Update in the appropriate list
    const updateList = (list: DeviceDescription[]) =>
      list.map((item) =>
        item.deviceKey === selectedItem.deviceKey ? updated : item
      );

    set({
      devices: updateList(devices),
      guides: updateList(guides),
      mechanics: updateList(mechanics),
    });
  },

  updateOperationalDetail: (index, detail) => {
    const { selectedItem } = get();
    if (!selectedItem) return;

    const details = [...(selectedItem.operationalDetails || [])];
    details[index] = detail;
    get().updateSelectedItem({ operationalDetails: details });
  },

  addOperationalDetail: (detail) => {
    const { selectedItem } = get();
    if (!selectedItem) return;

    const details = [...(selectedItem.operationalDetails || []), detail];
    get().updateSelectedItem({ operationalDetails: details });
  },

  removeOperationalDetail: (index) => {
    const { selectedItem } = get();
    if (!selectedItem) return;

    const details = [...(selectedItem.operationalDetails || [])];
    details.splice(index, 1);
    get().updateSelectedItem({ operationalDetails: details });
  },

  reorderOperationalDetails: (fromIndex, toIndex) => {
    const { selectedItem } = get();
    if (!selectedItem) return;

    const details = [...(selectedItem.operationalDetails || [])];
    const [removed] = details.splice(fromIndex, 1);
    details.splice(toIndex, 0, removed);
    get().updateSelectedItem({ operationalDetails: details });
  },

  reset: () => set(initialState),
}));

```

- newString:

```
/**
 * File service for handling file operations via Electron IPC
 */
import type { DeviceDescription } from '@types/stationpedia';

export interface FileResult<T = void> {
  success: boolean;
  data?: T;
  error?: string;
}

export interface WorkspaceData {
  descriptions: DeviceDescription[];
  guides: DeviceDescription[];
  mechanics: DeviceDescription[];
  assets: string[];
}

class FileService {
  private isElectron: boolean;

  constructor() {
    this.isElectron = typeof window !== 'undefined' && !!window.electronAPI;
  }

  /**
   * Open folder picker dialog
   */
  async openFolder(): Promise<string | null> {
    if (!this.isElectron) {
      console.warn('Electron API not available - using mock');
      return null;
    }
    return window.electronAPI.openFolder();
  }

  /**
   * Read a file and return its content
   */
  async readFile(filePath: string): Promise<FileResult<string>> {
    if (!this.isElectron) {
      return { success: false, error: 'Electron API not available' };
    }
    return window.electronAPI.readFile(filePath);
  }

  /**
   * Write content to a file with optional backup
   */
  async writeFile(filePath: string, content: string, createBackup = true): Promise<FileResult> {
    if (!this.isElectron) {
      return { success: false, error: 'Electron API not available' };
    }
    return window.electronAPI.writeFile(filePath, content, createBackup);
  }

  /**
   * List files in a directory with optional extension filter
   */
  async listFiles(directory: string, extensions: string[] = []): Promise<FileResult<string[]>> {
    if (!this.isElectron) {
      return { success: false, error: 'Electron API not available' };
    }
    return window.electronAPI.listFiles(directory, extensions);
  }

  /**
   * Check if a path exists
   */
  async exists(filePath: string): Promise<boolean> {
    if (!this.isElectron) {
      return false;
    }
    return window.electronAPI.exists(filePath);
  }

  /**
   * Get settings (last workspace, preferences, etc.)
   */
  async getSettings(): Promise<Record<string, any>> {
    if (!this.isElectron) {
      // Try localStorage fallback for web mode
      try {
        const stored = localStorage.getItem('stationpedia-editor-settings');
        return stored ? JSON.parse(stored) : {};
      } catch {
        return {};
      }
    }
    return window.electronAPI.getSettings();
  }

  /**
   * Save settings
   */
  async setSettings(settings: Record<string, any>): Promise<FileResult> {
    if (!this.isElectron) {
      try {
        localStorage.setItem('stationpedia-editor-settings', JSON.stringify(settings));
        return { success: true };
      } catch (error: any) {
        return { success: false, error: error.message };
      }
    }
    return window.electronAPI.setSettings(settings);
  }

  /**
   * Load workspace data from a mod folder
   */
  async loadWorkspace(folderPath: string): Promise<FileResult<WorkspaceData>> {
    const data: WorkspaceData = {
      descriptions: [],
      guides: [],
      mechanics: [],
      assets: [],
    };

    try {
      // Look for descriptions.json
      const descPath = `${folderPath}/descriptions.json`;
      if (await this.exists(descPath)) {
        const result = await this.readFile(descPath);
        if (result.success && result.data) {
          try {
            const parsed = JSON.parse(result.data);
            if (Array.isArray(parsed)) {
              data.descriptions = parsed;
            } else if (parsed.devices) {
              data.descriptions = parsed.devices;
            } else if (parsed.descriptions) {
              data.descriptions = parsed.descriptions;
            }
          } catch (e) {
            console.error('Failed to parse descriptions.json:', e);
          }
        }
      }

      // Look for guides folder or guides.json
      const guidesPath = `${folderPath}/guides`;
      if (await this.exists(guidesPath)) {
        const guidesResult = await this.listFiles(guidesPath, ['.json']);
        if (guidesResult.success && guidesResult.data) {
          for (const file of guidesResult.data) {
            const fileResult = await this.readFile(file);
            if (fileResult.success && fileResult.data) {
              try {
                const guide = JSON.parse(fileResult.data);
                if (guide.deviceKey) {
                  data.guides.push(guide);
                }
              } catch (e) {
                console.error(`Failed to parse guide ${file}:`, e);
              }
            }
          }
        }
      }

      // Look for game-mechanics folder
      const mechanicsPath = `${folderPath}/game-mechanics`;
      if (await this.exists(mechanicsPath)) {
        const mechanicsResult = await this.listFiles(mechanicsPath, ['.json']);
        if (mechanicsResult.success && mechanicsResult.data) {
          for (const file of mechanicsResult.data) {
            const fileResult = await this.readFile(file);
            if (fileResult.success && fileResult.data) {
              try {
                const mechanic = JSON.parse(fileResult.data);
                if (mechanic.deviceKey) {
                  data.mechanics.push(mechanic);
                }
              } catch (e) {
                console.error(`Failed to parse mechanic ${file}:`, e);
              }
            }
          }
        }
      }

      // Find assets
      const assetsPath = `${folderPath}/assets`;
      if (await this.exists(assetsPath)) {
        const assetsResult = await this.listFiles(assetsPath, ['.png', '.jpg', '.jpeg', '.gif']);
        if (assetsResult.success && assetsResult.data) {
          data.assets = assetsResult.data;
        }
      }

      return { success: true, data };
    } catch (error: any) {
      return { success: false, error: error.message };
    }
  }

  /**
   * Save descriptions.json
   */
  async saveDescriptions(folderPath: string, descriptions: DeviceDescription[]): Promise<FileResult> {
    const filePath = `${folderPath}/descriptions.json`;
    const content = JSON.stringify(descriptions, null, 2);
    return this.writeFile(filePath, content, true);
  }

  /**
   * Save a single guide or mechanic
   */
  async saveItem(folderPath: string, item: DeviceDescription, type: 'guide' | 'mechanic'): Promise<FileResult> {
    const subFolder = type === 'guide' ? 'guides' : 'game-mechanics';
    const filePath = `${folderPath}/${subFolder}/${item.deviceKey}.json`;
    const content = JSON.stringify(item, null, 2);
    return this.writeFile(filePath, content, true);
  }
}

export const fileService = new FileService();

```

 */

export interface FileServiceAPI {
  openFolder(): Promise<string | null>;
  readFile(path: string): Promise<string>;
  writeFile(path: string, content: string): Promise<void>;
  listFiles(directory: string, extension: string): Promise<string[]>;
}

// Mock implementation for development
export const fileService: FileServiceAPI = {
  async openFolder() {
    return null;
  },
  async readFile(path: string) {
    return '';
  },
  async writeFile(path: string, content: string) {

};

```
/**
 * Stationpedia type definitions
 * Matches the structure from descriptions.json
 */

export interface DeviceDescription {
  deviceKey: string;
  displayName?: string;
  pageDescription?: string;
  generateToc?: boolean;
  operationalDetails?: OperationalDetail[];
  logicDescriptions?: Record<string, LogicDescription>;
  slots?: SlotDefinition[];
  prefabs?: string[];
  tag?: string;
  // Allow unknown fields to round-trip
  [key: string]: unknown;
}

export interface OperationalDetail {
  title: string;
  tocId?: string;
  collapsible?: boolean;
  description?: string;
  items?: string[];
  steps?: string[];
  imageFile?: string;
  children?: OperationalDetail[];
  // Allow unknown fields
  [key: string]: unknown;
}

export interface LogicDescription {
  dataType: string;
  range: string;
  description: string;
  [key: string]: unknown;
}

export interface SlotDefinition {
  slotNumber: number;
  slotType: string;
  description?: string;
  [key: string]: unknown;
}

export interface ValidationError {
  type: 'error' | 'warning' | 'info';
  line?: number;
  message: string;
  field?: string;
  deviceKey?: string;
}

export interface EditorState {
  currentDevice: DeviceDescription | null;
  devices: DeviceDescription[];
  isDirty: boolean;
  validationErrors: ValidationError[];
}

// Content type categories
export type ContentType = 'device' | 'guide' | 'mechanic';

export interface ContentItem {
  type: ContentType;
  data: DeviceDescription;
  filePath?: string;
}

// Rich text AST types (TipTap-compatible)
export interface RichTextNode {
  type: string;
  attrs?: Record<string, unknown>;
  content?: RichTextNode[];
  marks?: RichTextMark[];
  text?: string;
}

export interface RichTextMark {
  type: string;
  attrs?: Record<string, unknown>;
}

// Link target for internal Stationpedia links
export interface LinkTarget {
  key: string;
  displayName: string;
  type: ContentType;
}

```

- newString:

```
import React, { useCallback, useEffect, useState } from 'react';
import { useEditor, EditorContent } from '@tiptap/react';
import StarterKit from '@tiptap/starter-kit';
import Link from '@tiptap/extension-link';
import Image from '@tiptap/extension-image';
import Table from '@tiptap/extension-table';
import TableRow from '@tiptap/extension-table-row';
import TableCell from '@tiptap/extension-table-cell';
import TableHeader from '@tiptap/extension-table-header';
import Underline from '@tiptap/extension-underline';
import Placeholder from '@tiptap/extension-placeholder';
import CharacterCount from '@tiptap/extension-character-count';
import type { DeviceDescription, LinkTarget } from '@types/stationpedia';
import { LinkPickerModal } from './LinkPickerModal';
import { useStore } from '@store/index';

interface EditorProps {
  device: DeviceDescription | null;
  onContentChange: (content: string) => void;
}

export const Editor: React.FC<EditorProps> = ({ device, onContentChange }) => {
  const [isSourceView, setIsSourceView] = useState(false);
  const [sourceContent, setSourceContent] = useState('');
  const [showLinkPicker, setShowLinkPicker] = useState(false);
  const { allContent } = useStore();

  const editor = useEditor({
    extensions: [
      StarterKit.configure({
        heading: {
          levels: [1, 2, 3],
        },
      }),
      Link.configure({
        openOnClick: false,
        HTMLAttributes: {
          class: 'text-stationpedia-accent underline cursor-pointer',
        },
      }),
      Image.configure({
        allowBase64: true,
      }),
      Table.configure({
        resizable: true,
      }),
      TableRow,
      TableCell,
      TableHeader,
      Underline,
      Placeholder.configure({
        placeholder: 'Start typing your content here...',
      }),
      CharacterCount,
    ],
    content: device?.pageDescription || '',
    editorProps: {
      attributes: {
        class: 'prose prose-invert max-w-none focus:outline-none min-h-full p-4',
      },
    },
    onUpdate: ({ editor }) => {
      const html = editor.getHTML();
      onContentChange(html);
      setSourceContent(html);
    },
  });

  // Update editor when device changes
  useEffect(() => {
    if (editor && device) {
      const content = device.pageDescription || '';
      editor.commands.setContent(content);
      setSourceContent(content);
    }
  }, [device?.deviceKey, editor]);

  // Handle source view changes
  const handleSourceChange = useCallback((e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLTextAreaElement>) => {
    const newContent = e.target.value;
    setSourceContent(newContent);
    onContentChange(newContent);
  }, [onContentChange]);

  // Apply source changes to editor when switching back
  const toggleSourceView = useCallback(() => {
    if (isSourceView && editor) {
      editor.commands.setContent(sourceContent);
    }
    setIsSourceView(!isSourceView);
  }, [isSourceView, sourceContent, editor]);

  // Handle link insertion from picker
  const handleLinkInsert = useCallback((target: LinkTarget) => {
    if (!editor) return;
    
    const linkHref = `stationpedia://${target.key}`;
    const linkText = target.displayName || target.key;
    
    editor
      .chain()
      .focus()
      .insertContent(`<a href="${linkHref}">{THING:${target.key}}</a>`)
      .run();
    
    setShowLinkPicker(false);
  }, [editor]);

  if (!editor && !isSourceView) return null;

  const ToolbarButton: React.FC<{
    onClick: () => void;
    active?: boolean;
    disabled?: boolean;
    children: React.ReactNode;
    title?: string;
  }> = ({ onClick, active, disabled, children, title }) => (
    <button
      onClick={onClick}
      disabled={disabled}
      title={title}
      className={`px-2 py-1 rounded text-sm font-medium transition-colors ${
        active
          ? 'bg-stationpedia-accent text-stationpedia-bg'
          : 'bg-stationpedia-border text-stationpedia-text hover:bg-stationpedia-border/80'
      } ${disabled ? 'opacity-50 cursor-not-allowed' : ''}`}
    >
      {children}
    </button>
  );

  const ToolbarDivider = () => (
    <div className="border-l border-stationpedia-border h-6 mx-1" />
  );

  return (
    <div className="flex-1 flex flex-col bg-stationpedia-surface border-r border-stationpedia-border min-w-0">
      {/* Toolbar */}
      <div className="bg-stationpedia-bg border-b border-stationpedia-border p-2 flex gap-1 flex-wrap items-center">
        {/* View Toggle */}
        <ToolbarButton onClick={toggleSourceView} active={isSourceView} title="Toggle source view">
          {isSourceView ? '📝 Visual' : '&lt;/&gt; Source'}
        </ToolbarButton>

        <ToolbarDivider />

        {/* Headings */}
        <ToolbarButton
          onClick={() => editor?.chain().focus().toggleHeading({ level: 1 }).run()}
          active={editor?.isActive('heading', { level: 1 })}
          disabled={isSourceView}
          title="Heading 1"
        >
          H1
        </ToolbarButton>
        <ToolbarButton
          onClick={() => editor?.chain().focus().toggleHeading({ level: 2 }).run()}
          active={editor?.isActive('heading', { level: 2 })}
          disabled={isSourceView}
          title="Heading 2"
        >
          H2
        </ToolbarButton>
        <ToolbarButton
          onClick={() => editor?.chain().focus().toggleHeading({ level: 3 }).run()}
          active={editor?.isActive('heading', { level: 3 })}
          disabled={isSourceView}
          title="Heading 3"
        >
          H3
        </ToolbarButton>

        <ToolbarDivider />

        {/* Text Format */}
        <ToolbarButton
          onClick={() => editor?.chain().focus().toggleBold().run()}
          active={editor?.isActive('bold')}
          disabled={isSourceView}
          title="Bold (Ctrl+B)"
        >
          <strong>B</strong>
        </ToolbarButton>
        <ToolbarButton
          onClick={() => editor?.chain().focus().toggleItalic().run()}
          active={editor?.isActive('italic')}
          disabled={isSourceView}
          title="Italic (Ctrl+I)"
        >
          <em>I</em>
        </ToolbarButton>
        <ToolbarButton
          onClick={() => editor?.chain().focus().toggleUnderline().run()}
          active={editor?.isActive('underline')}
          disabled={isSourceView}
          title="Underline (Ctrl+U)"
        >
          <u>U</u>
        </ToolbarButton>
        <ToolbarButton
          onClick={() => editor?.chain().focus().toggleCode().run()}
          active={editor?.isActive('code')}
          disabled={isSourceView}
          title="Inline code"
        >
          {'<>'}
        </ToolbarButton>

        <ToolbarDivider />

        {/* Lists */}
        <ToolbarButton
          onClick={() => editor?.chain().focus().toggleBulletList().run()}
          active={editor?.isActive('bulletList')}
          disabled={isSourceView}
          title="Bullet list"
        >
          • List
        </ToolbarButton>
        <ToolbarButton
          onClick={() => editor?.chain().focus().toggleOrderedList().run()}
          active={editor?.isActive('orderedList')}
          disabled={isSourceView}
          title="Numbered list"
        >
          1. List
        </ToolbarButton>

        <ToolbarDivider />

        {/* Links & Media */}
        <ToolbarButton
          onClick={() => setShowLinkPicker(true)}
          disabled={isSourceView}
          title="Insert Stationpedia link"
        >
          🔗 Link
        </ToolbarButton>
        <ToolbarButton
          onClick={() => editor?.chain().focus().insertTable({ rows: 3, cols: 3 }).run()}
          disabled={isSourceView}
          title="Insert table"
        >
          📊 Table
        </ToolbarButton>
      </div>

      {/* Editor Content */}
      <div className="flex-1 overflow-auto bg-stationpedia-bg">
        {isSourceView ? (
          <textarea
            value={sourceContent}
            onChange={handleSourceChange}
            className="w-full h-full p-4 bg-stationpedia-bg text-stationpedia-text font-mono text-sm resize-none focus:outline-none"
            spellCheck={false}
          />
        ) : (
          <EditorContent editor={editor} className="h-full" />
        )}
      </div>

      {/* Status Bar */}
      <div className="bg-stationpedia-surface border-t border-stationpedia-border px-4 py-2 flex justify-between text-xs text-stationpedia-text-muted">
        <span>
          {device?.displayName || device?.deviceKey || 'No device selected'}
        </span>
        <span>
          {editor?.storage.characterCount?.characters() || sourceContent.length} characters
        </span>
      </div>

      {/* Link Picker Modal */}
      {showLinkPicker && (
        <LinkPickerModal
          targets={allContent.map(item => ({
            key: item.deviceKey,
            displayName: item.displayName || item.deviceKey,
            type: 'device' as const,
          }))}
          onSelect={handleLinkInsert}
          onClose={() => setShowLinkPicker(false)}
        />
      )}
    </div>
  );
};
```

- oldString:

```
import React, { useRef, useEffect } from 'react';
import { useEditor, EditorContent } from '@tiptap/react';
import StarterKit from '@tiptap/starter-kit';
import Link from '@tiptap/extension-link';
import Image from '@tiptap/extension-image';
import Table from '@tiptap/extension-table';
import TableRow from '@tiptap/extension-table-row';
import TableCell from '@tiptap/extension-table-cell';
import type { DeviceDescription } from '@types/stationpedia';

interface EditorProps {
  device: DeviceDescription | null;
  onContentChange: (content: string) => void;
}

export const Editor: React.FC<EditorProps> = ({ device, onContentChange }) => {
  const editor = useEditor({
    extensions: [
      StarterKit,
      Link.configure({
        openOnClick: false,
      }),
      Image.configure({
        allowBase64: true,
      }),
      Table.configure({
        resizable: true,
      }),
      TableRow,
      TableCell,
    ],
    content: device?.pageDescription || '<p>Select a device to edit</p>',
    editorProps: {
      attributes: {
        class:
          'prose prose-invert max-w-none focus:outline-none text-stationpedia-text bg-stationpedia-bg p-4',
      },
    },
    onUpdate: ({ editor }) => {
      onContentChange(editor.getHTML());
    },
  });

  useEffect(() => {
    if (editor && device) {
      editor.commands.setContent(device.pageDescription || '');
    }
  }, [device, editor]);

  if (!editor) return null;

  return (
    <div className="flex-1 flex flex-col bg-stationpedia-surface border-r border-stationpedia-border">
      {/* Toolbar */}
      <div className="bg-stationpedia-bg border-b border-stationpedia-border p-2 flex gap-1 flex-wrap">
        {/* Text Format Buttons */}
        <button
          onClick={() => editor.chain().focus().toggleBold().run()}
          disabled={!editor.can().chain().focus().toggleBold().run()}
          className={`px-3 py-1 rounded text-sm transition-colors ${
            editor.isActive('bold')
              ? 'bg-stationpedia-accent text-stationpedia-bg'
              : 'bg-stationpedia-border text-stationpedia-text hover:bg-stationpedia-border/80'
          }`}
        >
          Bold
        </button>

        <button
          onClick={() => editor.chain().focus().toggleItalic().run()}
          disabled={!editor.can().chain().focus().toggleItalic().run()}
          className={`px-3 py-1 rounded text-sm transition-colors ${
            editor.isActive('italic')
              ? 'bg-stationpedia-accent text-stationpedia-bg'
              : 'bg-stationpedia-border text-stationpedia-text hover:bg-stationpedia-border/80'
          }`}
        >
          Italic
        </button>

        <button
          onClick={() => editor.chain().focus().toggleCodeBlock().run()}
          disabled={!editor.can().chain().focus().toggleCodeBlock().run()}
          className={`px-3 py-1 rounded text-sm transition-colors ${
            editor.isActive('codeBlock')
              ? 'bg-stationpedia-accent text-stationpedia-bg'
              : 'bg-stationpedia-border text-stationpedia-text hover:bg-stationpedia-border/80'
          }`}
        >
          Code
        </button>

        <div className="border-l border-stationpedia-border mx-1" />

        <button
          onClick={() => editor.chain().focus().toggleBulletList().run()}
          className={`px-3 py-1 rounded text-sm transition-colors ${
            editor.isActive('bulletList')
              ? 'bg-stationpedia-accent text-stationpedia-bg'
              : 'bg-stationpedia-border text-stationpedia-text hover:bg-stationpedia-border/80'
          }`}
        >
          List
        </button>

        <button
          onClick={() => editor.chain().focus().insertTable({ rows: 3, cols: 3 }).run()}
          className="px-3 py-1 rounded text-sm bg-stationpedia-border text-stationpedia-text hover:bg-stationpedia-border/80 transition-colors"
        >
          Table
        </button>
      </div>

      {/* Editor Content */}
      <div className="flex-1 overflow-auto bg-stationpedia-bg">
        <EditorContent editor={editor} />
      </div>

      {/* Character count */}
      <div className="bg-stationpedia-surface border-t border-stationpedia-border px-4 py-2 text-xs text-stationpedia-text-muted">
        {editor.storage.characterCount?.characters() || 0} characters
      </div>
    </div>
  );
};
```
