- newString:

```
  /**
   * Load workspace data from a mod folder
   */
  async loadWorkspace(folderPath: string): Promise<FileResult<WorkspaceData>> {
    const data: WorkspaceData = {
      descriptions: [],
      guides: [],
      mechanics: [],
      assets: [],
    };

    try {
      // Look for descriptions.json
      const descPath = `${folderPath}/descriptions.json`;
      if (await this.exists(descPath)) {
        const result = await this.readFile(descPath);
        if (result.success && result.data) {
          try {
            const parsed = JSON.parse(result.data);
            // Handle the Stationpedia Ascended format
            if (parsed.devices && Array.isArray(parsed.devices)) {
              // New format: { genericDescriptions: {...}, devices: [...] }
              data.descriptions = parsed.devices.map((d: any) => ({
                ...d,
                // Normalize OperationalDetails to operationalDetails
                operationalDetails: d.OperationalDetails || d.operationalDetails || [],
              }));
            } else if (Array.isArray(parsed)) {
              // Old format: direct array
              data.descriptions = parsed;
            } else if (parsed.descriptions) {
              // Alternative format
              data.descriptions = parsed.descriptions;
            }
          } catch (e) {
            console.error('Failed to parse descriptions.json:', e);
          }
        }
      }

      // Look for guides folder - supports both .json and .md files
      const guidesPath = `${folderPath}/guides`;
      const guidesPathAlt = `${folderPath}/Guides`; // Case-sensitive check
      const actualGuidesPath = await this.exists(guidesPathAlt) ? guidesPathAlt : 
                               await this.exists(guidesPath) ? guidesPath : null;
      
      if (actualGuidesPath) {
        const guidesResult = await this.listFiles(actualGuidesPath, ['.json', '.md']);
        if (guidesResult.success && guidesResult.data) {
          for (const file of guidesResult.data) {
            const fileResult = await this.readFile(file);
            if (fileResult.success && fileResult.data) {
              const ext = file.toLowerCase().split('.').pop();
              const fileName = file.split(/[/\\]/).pop() || '';
              const baseName = fileName.replace(/\.(json|md)$/i, '');
              
              if (ext === 'json') {
                try {
                  const guide = JSON.parse(fileResult.data);
                  if (guide.deviceKey) {
                    data.guides.push(guide);
                  }
                } catch (e) {
                  console.error(`Failed to parse guide ${file}:`, e);
                }
              } else if (ext === 'md') {
                // Convert markdown to a guide entry
                data.guides.push({
                  deviceKey: `Guide_${baseName.replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9]/g, '_')}`,
                  displayName: baseName.split('-').map(w => 
                    w.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + w.slice(1)
                  ).join(' '),
                  pageDescription: fileResult.data,
                  _sourcePath: file,
                  _isMarkdown: true,
                });
              }
            }
          }
        }
      }

      // Look for game-mechanics folder
      const mechanicsPath = `${folderPath}/game-mechanics`;
      if (await this.exists(mechanicsPath)) {
        const mechanicsResult = await this.listFiles(mechanicsPath, ['.json', '.md']);
        if (mechanicsResult.success && mechanicsResult.data) {
          for (const file of mechanicsResult.data) {
            const fileResult = await this.readFile(file);
            if (fileResult.success && fileResult.data) {
              const ext = file.toLowerCase().split('.').pop();
              const fileName = file.split(/[/\\]/).pop() || '';
              const baseName = fileName.replace(/\.(json|md)$/i, '');
              
              if (ext === 'json') {
                try {
                  const mechanic = JSON.parse(fileResult.data);
                  if (mechanic.deviceKey) {
                    data.mechanics.push(mechanic);
                  }
                } catch (e) {
                  console.error(`Failed to parse mechanic ${file}:`, e);
                }
              } else if (ext === 'md') {
                data.mechanics.push({
                  deviceKey: `Mechanic_${baseName.replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9]/g, '_')}`,
                  displayName: baseName.split('-').map(w => 
                    w.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + w.slice(1)
                  ).join(' '),
                  pageDescription: fileResult.data,
                  _sourcePath: file,
                  _isMarkdown: true,
                });
              }
            }
          }
        }
      }

      // Find assets
      const assetsPath = `${folderPath}/assets`;
      const imagesPath = `${folderPath}/images`; // Alt path used by the mod
      const actualAssetsPath = await this.exists(imagesPath) ? imagesPath :
                               await this.exists(assetsPath) ? assetsPath : null;
      
      if (actualAssetsPath) {
        const assetsResult = await this.listFiles(actualAssetsPath, ['.png', '.jpg', '.jpeg', '.gif']);
        if (assetsResult.success && assetsResult.data) {
          data.assets = assetsResult.data;
        }
      }

      return { success: true, data };
    } catch (error: any) {
      return { success: false, error: error.message };
    }
  }
```

- oldString:

  /**
   * Load workspace data from a mod folder
   */
  async loadWorkspace(folderPath: string): Promise<FileResult<WorkspaceData>> {
    const data: WorkspaceData = {
      descriptions: [],
      guides: [],
      mechanics: [],
      assets: [],
    };

    try {
      // Look for descriptions.json
      const descPath = `${folderPath}/descriptions.json`;
      if (await this.exists(descPath)) {
        const result = await this.readFile(descPath);
        if (result.success && result.data) {
          try {
            const parsed = JSON.parse(result.data);
            if (Array.isArray(parsed)) {
              data.descriptions = parsed;
            } else if (parsed.devices) {
              data.descriptions = parsed.devices;
            } else if (parsed.descriptions) {
              data.descriptions = parsed.descriptions;
            }
          } catch (e) {
            console.error('Failed to parse descriptions.json:', e);
          }
        }
      }

      // Look for guides folder or guides.json
      const guidesPath = `${folderPath}/guides`;
      if (await this.exists(guidesPath)) {
        const guidesResult = await this.listFiles(guidesPath, ['.json']);
        if (guidesResult.success && guidesResult.data) {
          for (const file of guidesResult.data) {
            const fileResult = await this.readFile(file);
            if (fileResult.success && fileResult.data) {
              try {
                const guide = JSON.parse(fileResult.data);
                if (guide.deviceKey) {
                  data.guides.push(guide);
                }
              } catch (e) {
                console.error(`Failed to parse guide ${file}:`, e);
              }
            }
          }
        }
      }

      // Look for game-mechanics folder
      const mechanicsPath = `${folderPath}/game-mechanics`;
      if (await this.exists(mechanicsPath)) {
        const mechanicsResult = await this.listFiles(mechanicsPath, ['.json']);
        if (mechanicsResult.success && mechanicsResult.data) {
          for (const file of mechanicsResult.data) {
            const fileResult = await this.readFile(file);
            if (fileResult.success && fileResult.data) {
              try {
                const mechanic = JSON.parse(fileResult.data);
                if (mechanic.deviceKey) {
                  data.mechanics.push(mechanic);
                }
              } catch (e) {
                console.error(`Failed to parse mechanic ${file}:`, e);
              }
            }
          }
        }
      }

      // Find assets
      const assetsPath = `${folderPath}/assets`;
      if (await this.exists(assetsPath)) {
        const assetsResult = await this.listFiles(assetsPath, ['.png', '.jpg', '.jpeg', '.gif']);
        if (assetsResult.success && assetsResult.data) {
          data.assets = assetsResult.data;
        }
      }

      return { success: true, data };
    } catch (error: any) {
      return { success: false, error: error.message };
    }
  }

- newString:

```
  /**
   * Save descriptions.json (preserves the original format structure)
   */
  async saveDescriptions(folderPath: string, descriptions: DeviceDescription[]): Promise<FileResult> {
    const filePath = `${folderPath}/descriptions.json`;
    
    // First read the existing file to preserve the structure (genericDescriptions, etc)
    const existingResult = await this.readFile(filePath);
    let fileContent: any;
    
    if (existingResult.success && existingResult.data) {
      try {
        const existing = JSON.parse(existingResult.data);
        if (existing.devices) {
          // Preserve existing structure, just update devices
          // Also convert operationalDetails back to OperationalDetails for consistency
          fileContent = {
            ...existing,
            devices: descriptions.map(d => {
              const { operationalDetails, ...rest } = d as any;
              return {
                ...rest,
                OperationalDetails: operationalDetails || d.operationalDetails,
              };
            }),
          };
        } else {
          // Direct array format
          fileContent = descriptions;
        }
      } catch {
        fileContent = descriptions;
      }
    } else {
      fileContent = descriptions;
    }
    
    const content = JSON.stringify(fileContent, null, 2);
    return this.writeFile(filePath, content, true);
  }
```

- oldString:

```
  /**
   * Save descriptions.json
   */
  async saveDescriptions(folderPath: string, descriptions: DeviceDescription[]): Promise<FileResult> {
    const filePath = `${folderPath}/descriptions.json`;
    const content = JSON.stringify(descriptions, null, 2);
    return this.writeFile(filePath, content, true);
  }
```

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

Great question! Let me clarify the current design and workflow:

## Current Layout

The editor has **two distinct sections** for editing different parts of a Stationpedia entry:

### 1. Top Section: Main Editor (Page Description)
This is the **rich text editor** with the toolbar (bold, italic, headings, links, etc.). It edits the `pageDescription` field - the main descriptive text that appears at the top of a Stationpedia page before any collapsible sections.

### 2. Bottom Section: Operational Details Editor  
This is a **structured data editor** for the `operationalDetails` array - the collapsible sections that appear in-game. Each section has:
- **Title** - The header text
- **Description** - Supporting text (currently plain textarea)
- **Items** - Bullet point lists
- **Steps** - Numbered procedure lists
- **Children** - Nested sub-sections

## The Problem You've Identified

You're right that there's a disconnect - the Operational Details editor only has plain textareas for description text, so you lose the rich formatting tools. Currently if you want formatted text in an operational detail's description, you'd need to type raw HTML/TMP tags manually.

## Options

1. **Keep as-is**: The Page Description gets rich editing, Operational Details are simpler structured sections (how it currently works)

2. **Add rich editing to Operational Details**: I can enhance the OperationalDetailsEditor so each description field also gets a mini TipTap editor with formatting tools

3. **Single unified editor**: Restructure so everything is in one editor view

Would you like me to add rich text editing to the Operational Details descriptions? That would give you consistent formatting tools throughout the entire page content.

Please do the following. Don't stop to ask questions unless absolutely necessary. Please build to completion. Don't worry about github pushing but please use your conductor instructions other wise. 

You are a senior full-stack engineer and tools architect.

Build a desktop application that functions as an IDE for authoring and validating the
Stationeers mod “Stationpedia Ascended”.

This tool is NOT a JSON editor and NOT a Unity emulator.

IMPORTANT CLARIFICATION:
• All edits MUST ultimately be written to the mod’s JSON files.
• The mod reads JSON directly.
• HOWEVER: the user must NEVER be required to manually edit JSON as their primary workflow.
• JSON is a persistence/output format, not the authoring interface.

All user edits occur via GUI and structured editors.
The application automatically serializes those edits back into mod-compatible JSON.

────────────────────────────────────────────
PRIMARY INTENT
────────────────────────────────────────────

The purpose of this application is to let the author:

• Edit Devices, Gameplay Guides, Game Mechanics, Operational Details, and Tooltips
• See exactly how those edits will appear in-game
• Make changes without touching code or manually editing JSON
• Save changes that directly update the mod’s files so the game loads them as-is

The mod’s JSON files are the single source of truth.
The editor must be lossless and must never discard or corrupt data.

────────────────────────────────────────────
CORE DESIGN PRINCIPLE (CRITICAL)
────────────────────────────────────────────

Reimplement Stationpedia’s rendering RULES, not Unity itself.

You are building a deterministic Stationpedia Renderer using HTML/CSS/JS that:
• Uses the same PNG assets, icons, backgrounds, and layout logic as the game
• Supports the same navigation model (home, back, forward, search, mode toggle)
• Produces a functionally and visually faithful preview

Pixel-perfect Unity reproduction is NOT required.
Functional and structural parity IS required.

────────────────────────────────────────────
APPLICATION ARCHITECTURE (MANDATORY)
────────────────────────────────────────────

The application consists of TWO coordinated systems:

1) EDITOR APPLICATION (Authoring IDE)
2) STATIONPEDIA SIMULATOR (Live Preview Window)

These must stay in sync via shared in-memory state.

────────────────────────────────────────────
EDITOR APPLICATION REQUIREMENTS
────────────────────────────────────────────

• Word-processor-style rich text editing
• Drag-and-drop reordering of nested sections and blocks
• Visual editing for:
  – Guides
  – Device pages
  – Game mechanics
  – Operational details
  – Tooltips
• Asset picker for images and videos
• Internal link picker (no manual ID typing)
• Optional source view for power users (clearly labeled)

PANELS & WINDOWS:
• Panels must be resizable
• Panels must be dockable
• Panels must be pop-out capable into separate windows

EDITOR UI VISUAL STYLE:
• Use Stationeers IC10 / Notepad colors:
  – Rounded window corners
  – Orange edge accents
  – Light blue background
  – Dark blue text panels

This styling applies ONLY to the editor, not the simulator.

────────────────────────────────────────────
STATIONPEDIA SIMULATOR WINDOW
────────────────────────────────────────────

• Separate window from the editor
• Renders pages using the Stationpedia Renderer
• Uses game assets and styling
• Fully interactive:
  – Buttons
  – Search
  – Navigation
  – Mode switching (Vanilla ↔ Stationpedia Ascended)
• Updates live as edits occur (debounced)

This window should feel like using Stationpedia inside the game.

────────────────────────────────────────────
COMPLETE DATA COVERAGE (MANDATORY)
────────────────────────────────────────────

The editor MUST display and preserve 100% of the data from Stationpedia JSON.

This includes (but is not limited to):
• Logic types
• Slots
• Images
• Icons
• Categories
• Metadata
• Device identifiers
• Any current or future fields used by the mod or game

The application must be LOSSLESS:
• Unknown or unmodeled fields must round-trip unchanged
• Field ordering should be preserved where practical
• No data may be dropped due to missing UI

────────────────────────────────────────────
CONTENT MODEL (CRITICAL)
────────────────────────────────────────────

Internally, represent pages as a structured AST, not raw strings.

• Known fields get structured editors
• Unknown fields are preserved verbatim

Every page MUST include an “All Fields / Inspector” panel:
• Displays the full JSON object for that page
• Auto-generated editable controls for unmodeled fields
• Optional raw JSON editor as a fallback (advanced users only)
• Changes in structured UI and inspector stay in sync

RAW JSON VIEW RULE:
• Raw JSON is a secondary, optional interface
• It must serialize through the same validation pipeline
• It must never bypass safety checks

────────────────────────────────────────────
SERIALIZATION RULES
────────────────────────────────────────────

• Write JSON in the exact format expected by the mod
• Preserve unknown fields
• Preserve ordering where possible
• Never normalize or reformat unnecessarily
• Automatically create backups before overwriting files

────────────────────────────────────────────
FILE SYSTEM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────

• User selects a mod workspace folder
• App indexes existing guides/devices/mechanics folders
• If structure is incompatible, implement a migration strategy
• Changes write directly to mod files
• Autosave + manual save supported

────────────────────────────────────────────
PERFORMANCE REQUIREMENTS
────────────────────────────────────────────

• All content indexed in memory
• Instant search and filtering
• No lag during editing
• Renderer updates debounced but live

────────────────────────────────────────────
NON-GOALS (DO NOT DO THESE)
────────────────────────────────────────────

• Do NOT emulate Unity internals
• Do NOT require users to write JSON
• Do NOT invent new content formats without mapping them to mod formats

────────────────────────────────────────────
IMPLEMENTATION PRIORITY ORDER
────────────────────────────────────────────

1) Lossless content AST + serialization
2) Stationpedia Renderer (HTML-based)
3) Editor UI with drag-and-drop
4) Simulator window with navigation
5) Tooltip editing system
6) Visual polish & theming

────────────────────────────────────────────
DELIVERABLES
────────────────────────────────────────────

Provide:
1) Architecture overview
2) Step-by-step implementation plan
3) Full working code (multi-file)
4) Local run/build instructions
5) Documented assumptions
6) TODO list for next enhancements

────────────────────────────────────────────
DEFINITION OF SUCCESS
────────────────────────────────────────────

• I can design an entire Stationpedia update without touching code
• The simulator preview matches what appears in-game
• The mod loads the generated files without modification

If anything is ambiguous:
• Make reasonable engineering assumptions
• Document them clearly
• Prefer correctness and maintainability over cleverness

_**User (2026-01-16 22:52:00Z)**_

This plan is very strong and generally approved. I want to proceed with it, but I need to clarify and adjust a few points to avoid unnecessary complexity and long-term risk. Please treat the following as authoritative refinements, not optional suggestions.

GENERAL DIRECTION (CONFIRMED)
• The phase order is correct.
• AST-first, lossless round-tripping is non-negotiable.
• JSON is the persistence format; users never edit JSON directly.
• Dual-window architecture (Editor + Simulator) is correct.

––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––
IMPORTANT REFINEMENTS
––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––

1) TMP SERIALIZATION REQUIREMENT (VERY IMPORTANT)
When serializing AST → TMP:
• Do NOT require byte-for-byte identical strings.
• The requirement is FUNCTIONAL and SEMANTIC equivalence.
• Output must render identically in-game and preserve all IDs, links, and structure.
• Preserve original raw TMP strings for unparsed or unknown constructs when necessary.

In short: prioritize correctness and safety over perfect string identity.

2) JSON FIELD ORDERING
• Preserve ordering where practical, especially for arrays.
• Do not block progress or add excessive complexity trying to guarantee object key order.
• Backups + lossless field preservation are the safety net.

3) STATIONPEDIA RENDERER SCOPE
• We are NOT emulating Unity.
• The renderer must reimplement Stationpedia’s rendering rules deterministically in HTML/CSS/JS.
• Use game PNG assets, colors, spacing, and layout logic.
• “Close enough but structurally accurate” is the goal.
• Do NOT attempt pixel-perfect or Unity-internal behavior.

4) ASSETS & SPRITES
• Using PNGs as backgrounds/icons is required.
• Do your best to implement the sprite slicing etc.


5) COMPLETE DATA RENDERING (CRITICAL)
• The renderer must display the FULL Stationpedia page object, not just text sections.
• This includes logic types, slots, images, metadata, and any other fields used by the game.
• The simulator preview must reflect these fields where they affect presentation.

6) DRAG-AND-DROP
• Use a tree-safe drag-and-drop system (e.g., @dnd-kit with nested strategy).
• Drag handles must move entire nodes including their children.
• This is not optional.

7) VISUAL REGRESSION TESTING
• Do NOT start with screenshot regression tests.
• First add structural DOM tests for renderer output.
• Add screenshot tests later once visual styling stabilizes.

8) CROSS-WINDOW STATE SYNC
• Do NOT rely on broadcast-channel as the primary sync mechanism.
• Use Electron IPC as the authoritative sync layer.
• Each window may use Zustand locally, but state changes must propagate via IPC events/patches.

––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––
OPEN QUESTIONS – DECISIONS
––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––

• Panel system: Proceed with react-mosaic-component.
• Rich text editor: Keep TipTap, but treat TipTap JSON as an implementation detail.
  The AST is the canonical model.
• Mode toggle behavior: Vanilla mode hides Ascended overrides; Ascended mode shows full mod content.

––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––
NEXT STEPS
––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––

Proceed with all phases to completion

If any ambiguity arises:
• Make conservative engineering assumptions.
• Document them.
• Prefer lossless behavior and maintainability over cleverness.

```
## Plan: Stationpedia Ascended IDE

Build a comprehensive IDE for authoring and validating the Stationeers mod "Stationpedia Ascended" with lossless JSON round-tripping, HTML-based Stationpedia rendering, and dual-window architecture.

**Phases: 7**

### Phase 1: Content AST & Lossless Serialization
- **Objective:** Build foundation with Content AST that perfectly round-trips JSON
- **Files/Functions:**
  - `src/renderer/models/ast.ts` - AST node types
  - `src/renderer/services/parser.ts` - TMP string → AST
  - `src/renderer/services/serializer.ts` - AST → TMP string  
  - `src/renderer/services/jsonCodec.ts` - JSON ↔ internal model
  - `src/renderer/models/contentModel.ts` - Full device/guide/mechanic types
- **Tests:** parser.test.ts, serializer.test.ts, roundtrip.test.ts
- **Key Requirements:**
  - Functional/semantic equivalence (not byte-for-byte)
  - Preserve unknown fields verbatim
  - Preserve array ordering

### Phase 2: Stationpedia Renderer (HTML)
- **Objective:** HTML/CSS renderer matching in-game appearance
- **Files/Functions:**
  - `src/renderer/simulator/StationpediaRenderer.tsx`
  - `src/renderer/simulator/components/` - CollapsibleSection, TOC, etc.
  - `src/renderer/simulator/styles/stationpedia.css`
- **Tests:** Structural DOM tests (screenshots later)
- **Key Requirements:**
  - Reimplement rendering RULES, not Unity
  - Use game PNG assets
  - Render ALL fields (logic, slots, images, metadata)

### Phase 3: Editor UI with Drag-and-Drop
- **Objective:** Authoring IDE with dockable panels
- **Files/Functions:**
  - `src/renderer/editor/PanelSystem.tsx` - react-mosaic
  - `src/renderer/editor/ContentTree.tsx`
  - `src/renderer/editor/RichTextEditor.tsx` - TipTap with AST
  - `src/renderer/editor/PropertyInspector.tsx`
  - `src/renderer/editor/OperationalDetailsTree.tsx` - @dnd-kit
- **Key Requirements:**
  - @dnd-kit with nested strategy
  - Drag handles move entire nodes + children
  - IC10/Notepad theming

### Phase 4: Simulator Window
- **Objective:** Separate preview window with navigation
- **Files/Functions:**
  - `src/main/simulatorWindow.ts`
  - `src/renderer/simulator/SimulatorApp.tsx`
  - `src/renderer/simulator/NavigationBar.tsx`
  - `src/renderer/services/sharedState.ts` - IPC-based sync
- **Key Requirements:**
  - Electron IPC as authoritative sync layer
  - Mode toggle (Vanilla ↔ Ascended)

### Phase 5: Tooltip Editing System
- **Objective:** Edit genericDescriptions tooltips
- **Files/Functions:**
  - `src/renderer/editor/TooltipEditor.tsx`
  - `src/renderer/simulator/TooltipPreview.tsx`

### Phase 6: Validation & Assets
- **Objective:** Comprehensive validation and asset management
- **Files/Functions:**
  - `src/renderer/services/validator.ts` - Enhanced
  - `src/renderer/editor/AssetBrowser.tsx`

### Phase 7: Polish & Persistence
- **Objective:** Final polish and documentation
- **Files/Functions:**
  - `src/renderer/services/persistence.ts`
  - User documentation

**Decisions Made:**
- Panel library: react-mosaic-component
- Rich text: TipTap with AST as canonical model
- State sync: Electron IPC authoritative, Zustand local
- Mode toggle: Vanilla hides Ascended overrides

**Open Questions:** None - all resolved.

```

## Phase 1: Content AST & Lossless Serialization

You are implementing Phase 1 of the Stationpedia Ascended IDE. Work autonomously and implement everything to completion.

### Objective
Build the foundation - a Content AST that preserves all data through JSON round-trips.

### Context
The existing project is at `c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaEditor\`.
The mod files are at `c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaAscended\mod\`.

Read the existing files to understand current structure:
- `src/renderer/models/stationpedia.ts` - Current types
- `src/renderer/services/fileService.ts` - Current file handling

### Files to Create/Modify

1. **`src/renderer/models/ast.ts`** - AST node types for TMP rich text
   ```typescript
   // Define all AST node types:
   // - TextNode, ParagraphNode
   // - BoldNode, ItalicNode, UnderlineNode, StrikethroughNode
   // - ColorNode (with hex color)
   // - LinkNode (for {THING:Key} and {LINK:Page;Text})
   // - CodeBlockNode (for MIPS code with <mspace>)
   // - BulletListNode, NumberedListNode
   // - ImageNode, VideoNode, YouTubeLinkNode
   ```

2. **`src/renderer/services/parser.ts`** - TMP string → AST
   - Parse TMP tags: `<color=#HEX>`, `<b>`, `<i>`, `<u>`, `<s>`, `<link=id>`, `<mspace=...>`
   - Parse special tags: `{THING:DeviceKey}`, `{LINK:Page;Text}`
   - Handle nested tags properly
   - Preserve unknown/unparsable content as raw text nodes

3. **`src/renderer/services/serializer.ts`** - AST → TMP string
   - Convert AST back to TMP format
   - Output must render identically in-game (functional equivalence, not byte-identical)
   - Preserve structure, IDs, and links

4. **`src/renderer/models/contentModel.ts`** - Complete device/guide types
   ```typescript
   // Device with ALL fields:
   interface DeviceDocument {
     deviceKey: string;
     displayName?: string;
     pageDescription?: string;
     pageDescriptionPrepend?: string;
     pageDescriptionAppend?: string;
     operationalDetails?: OperationalDetail[];
     OperationalDetails?: OperationalDetail[]; // Handle both cases
     operationalDetailsTitleColor?: string;
     generateToc?: boolean;
     tocTitle?: string;
     operationalDetailsBackgroundColor?: string;
     logicDescriptions?: Record<string, LogicDescription>;
     modeDescriptions?: Record<string, ModeDescription>;
     slotDescriptions?: Record<string, SlotDescription>;
     versionDescriptions?: Record<string, VersionDescription>;
     memoryDescriptions?: Record<string, MemoryDescription>;
     // Preserve unknown fields
     [key: string]: unknown;
   }
   ```

5. **`src/renderer/services/jsonCodec.ts`** - JSON ↔ internal model
   - `parseWorkspace(folderPath)` - Load and parse all content
   - `serializeWorkspace(model)` - Write back to JSON files
   - Preserve unknown fields verbatim
   - Preserve array ordering
   - Normalize `OperationalDetails` ↔ `operationalDetails`
   - Create backups before writing

### TMP Tags to Support
```
<color=#RRGGBB>text</color>
<b>text</b>
<i>text</i>
<u>text</u>
<s>text</s>
<link=tocId>text</link>
<mspace=0.5em>code</mspace>
{THING:PrefabKey}           → Auto-link to device
{LINK:PageKey;Display Text} → Manual link
Newlines (\n)
```

### Operational Detail Structure
```typescript
interface OperationalDetail {
  title: string;
  description?: string;           // Rich text (parse to AST)
  children?: OperationalDetail[]; // Nested sections
  items?: string[];               // Bullet list
  steps?: string[];               // Numbered list
  collapsible?: boolean;
  tocId?: string;
  imageFile?: string;
  backgroundColor?: string;
  youtubeUrl?: string;
  youtubeLabel?: string;
  videoFile?: string;
  [key: string]: unknown;         // Preserve unknowns
}
```

### Critical Requirements
1. **Lossless round-trip**: Load JSON → internal model → save JSON must preserve ALL data
2. **Unknown field preservation**: Any field not explicitly modeled must round-trip unchanged
3. **Functional equivalence**: Serialized TMP must render identically in-game (not byte-identical)
4. **Normalize field names**: Handle both `OperationalDetails` and `operationalDetails`

### Testing Approach
After implementing, test by:
1. Loading `descriptions.json` from the mod
2. Serializing back to JSON
3. Comparing to ensure no data loss (except whitespace/formatting)

### Return
When complete, return a summary of:
- Files created/modified
- Key functions implemented
- Any assumptions made
- Known limitations
```


</details></tool-use>

## Code Review: Phase 1 - Content AST & Lossless Serialization

Review the Phase 1 implementation for the Stationpedia Ascended IDE.

### Files to Review
Located at `c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaEditor\src\renderer\`:

1. `models/ast.ts` - AST node type definitions
2. `services/parser.ts` - TMP string → AST parser
3. `services/serializer.ts` - AST → TMP string serializer
4. `models/contentModel.ts` - Device/guide/mechanic types
5. `services/jsonCodec.ts` - JSON ↔ internal model codec

### Review Criteria

1. **Correctness**
   - Do AST types cover all TMP tags? (`<color>`, `<b>`, `<i>`, `<u>`, `<s>`, `<link>`, `<mspace>`, `{THING:}`, `{LINK:}`)
   - Does parser handle nested tags correctly?
   - Does serializer output functionally equivalent TMP?
   - Does JSON codec preserve unknown fields?

2. **Lossless Round-trip**
   - Can we load descriptions.json → model → save without data loss?
   - Are `OperationalDetails` and `operationalDetails` both handled?
   - Are unknown fields preserved verbatim?

3. **Code Quality**
   - TypeScript types are complete and strict
   - Error handling is appropriate
   - Code is maintainable and readable

4. **Test Coverage**
   - Are edge cases covered (empty strings, deeply nested tags, malformed input)?
   - Are round-trip tests comprehensive?

### Return Format
Return a structured review:
```
Status: APPROVED | NEEDS_REVISION | FAILED

Summary: Brief description

Issues:
- Issue 1 (severity: critical/major/minor)
- Issue 2 (severity)

Recommendations:
- Recommendation 1
- Recommendation 2
```

If NEEDS_REVISION or FAILED, clearly specify what must be fixed.
```


</details></tool-use>

```
## Fix Phase 1 Review Issues

The code review identified critical issues that must be fixed. Work autonomously to fix ALL of them.

### Location
`c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaEditor\src\renderer\`

### Issues to Fix

#### 1. [CRITICAL] TypeScript Path Issues in Tests
The test files may have import path issues. Check and fix:
- Ensure imports use correct relative paths or path aliases
- Add explicit type annotations to lambda parameters

#### 2. [CRITICAL] Incomplete Link Serialization

In `services/serializer.ts`, the `serializeInternalLink` function is broken:
- It ignores the `text` field for `{THING:}` links
- It doesn't handle `{LINK:PageKey;DisplayText}` format
- The fallback produces invalid output

**Fix by:**
1. Add an `originalFormat` field to `InternalLinkNode` in `models/ast.ts`:
   ```typescript
   interface InternalLinkNode {
     type: 'internalLink';
     target: string;      // The device/page key
     text?: string;       // Display text (optional)
     originalFormat: 'thing' | 'link' | 'html'; // Preserve format
   }
   ```

2. Update parser to set `originalFormat` based on what was parsed
3. Update serializer to respect `originalFormat`:
   - `thing` → `{THING:target}`
   - `link` → `{LINK:target;text}`
   - `html` → `<link=target>text</link>`

#### 3. [MAJOR] Video/PageBreak Nodes
Either:
- Implement parsing for `video` nodes, OR
- Remove unused types from the AST union

For now, remove `video` and `pageBreak` from the type union if not implemented.

#### 4. [MAJOR] Add Real Integration Test
Add a test that:
1. Loads a real excerpt from `descriptions.json`
2. Parses it to AST
3. Serializes back to TMP
4. Verifies `{THING:}` and `{LINK:}` tags are preserved

### Verification
After fixing:
1. Run `npm run type-check` - must pass with 0 errors
2. Run tests if configured - all must pass
3. Test with a real descriptions.json sample

### Return
Report what was fixed and confirm all issues are resolved.
```


</details></tool-use>

```
## Phase 1 Complete: Content AST & Lossless Serialization

The foundational AST layer for TMP (TextMesh Pro) rich text has been implemented with full lossless round-tripping. All parsing, serialization, and JSON codec functionality is working correctly with comprehensive test coverage.

**Files created/changed:**
- src/renderer/models/ast.ts
- src/renderer/models/contentModel.ts
- src/renderer/services/parser.ts
- src/renderer/services/serializer.ts
- src/renderer/services/jsonCodec.ts
- src/renderer/models/ast.test.ts
- src/renderer/services/parser.test.ts
- src/renderer/services/serializer.test.ts
- src/renderer/services/jsonCodec.test.ts
- src/renderer/services/integration.test.ts

**Functions created/changed:**
- AST Node Types: TextNode, ParagraphNode, BoldNode, ItalicNode, UnderlineNode, StrikethroughNode, ColorNode, InternalLinkNode, ExternalLinkNode, CodeBlockNode, BulletListNode, NumberedListNode, ImageNode, YouTubeLinkNode
- Factory Functions: createTextNode, createBoldNode, createItalicNode, createColorNode, createLinkNode, createBulletListNode, createNumberedListNode, createImageNode, createYouTubeLinkNode
- Parser: parseToAST, parseNodes, parseTag, parseSpecialTag, extractTagName, extractTagAttributes
- Serializer: serializeToTMP, serializeNode (with originalFormat preservation)
- JSON Codec: parseWorkspaceJSON, serializeWorkspaceJSON, parseDeviceDocument, serializeDeviceDocument
- Utilities: normalizeHex, isLeafNode, extractText, normalizeDeviceFields, denormalizeDeviceFields

**Tests created/changed:**
- ast.test.ts: 13 tests for AST node creation and utilities
- parser.test.ts: 20 tests for TMP string parsing
- serializer.test.ts: 22 tests for AST to TMP serialization
- jsonCodec.test.ts: 16 tests for JSON round-trip codec
- integration.test.ts: 12 tests with real descriptions.json excerpts

**Review Status:** APPROVED

**Key Implementation Details:**
1. **13 AST Node Types**: Full coverage of TMP formatting including bold, italic, underline, strikethrough, color, links (internal/external), code blocks, lists, images, and YouTube embeds
2. **Original Format Preservation**: InternalLinkNode stores `originalFormat?: 'thing' | 'link' | 'html'` to preserve serialization format ({THING:}, {LINK:}, or <link>)
3. **Field Name Normalization**: JSON codec handles `OperationalDetails` ↔ `operationalDetails` conversion automatically
4. **Unknown Field Preservation**: All unknown JSON fields are preserved through round-trips via index signatures
5. **Color Normalization**: Colors normalized to uppercase hex (#FF0000) internally

**Git Commit Message:**
```
feat: Add Content AST & lossless serialization layer

- Add 13 AST node types for TMP rich text representation
- Implement TMP string parser with tag support (<b>, <i>, <u>, <s>, <color>, <link>, <mspace>, {THING:}, {LINK:})
- Implement AST serializer with originalFormat preservation for links
- Add JSON codec with field name normalization and unknown field preservation
- Add comprehensive test suite (83 tests passing)
```

```

## Phase 2: Stationpedia Renderer (HTML)

You are implementing Phase 2 of the Stationpedia Ascended IDE. Work autonomously to completion.

### Objective
Build an HTML/CSS renderer that matches the in-game Stationpedia appearance. This will be used in the Simulator preview window.

### Context
- Workspace: `c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaEditor`
- Phase 1 is complete with AST types in `src/renderer/models/ast.ts` and content model in `src/renderer/models/contentModel.ts`
- The renderer should parse AST nodes and render them as styled HTML

### Files to Create/Modify

1. **`src/renderer/simulator/StationpediaRenderer.tsx`**
   - Main renderer component that takes a DeviceDocument and renders it
   - Import AST types and use parser/serializer from Phase 1
   - Render page description (parsed from TMP to AST, then to HTML)
   - Render all operational details sections
   - Support collapsible sections
   - Generate and render Table of Contents (TOC)

2. **`src/renderer/simulator/components/CollapsibleSection.tsx`**
   - Collapsible section component with expand/collapse animation
   - Match game styling with dark background

3. **`src/renderer/simulator/components/TOCPanel.tsx`**
   - Table of Contents panel component
   - Auto-generate from operational details with tocId
   - Smooth scroll navigation

4. **`src/renderer/simulator/components/DeviceHeader.tsx`**
   - Device name/title header
   - Display name with game-like styling

5. **`src/renderer/simulator/components/RichTextRenderer.tsx`**
   - Renders AST nodes to HTML/React elements
   - Support all node types: bold, italic, underline, strikethrough, color, links, lists, images, code blocks, YouTube embeds

6. **`src/renderer/simulator/components/OperationalDetailSection.tsx`**
   - Render a single operational detail section
   - Handle title, description, items, steps, children (recursive)
   - Support background colors, images

7. **`src/renderer/simulator/components/LogicSection.tsx`**
   - Render logic descriptions in table format
   - Show data type, range, description

8. **`src/renderer/simulator/styles/stationpedia.css`**
   - CSS matching game appearance
   - Dark theme (#1a1a2e background, light text)
   - Stationpedia-style fonts (monospace for code, sans-serif for content)
   - Collapsible section styling
   - TOC styling
   - Link colors (cyan for internal links)

### Key Requirements

1. **Render ALL device fields:**
   - pageDescription, pageDescriptionPrepend, pageDescriptionAppend
   - operationalDetails (recursive with children)
   - logicDescriptions (table format)
   - modeDescriptions, slotDescriptions, versionDescriptions
   - Images (imageFile field)
   - YouTube embeds (youtubeUrl, youtubeLabel)

2. **Styling must match game appearance:**
   - Dark background (#1a1a2e or similar)
   - Section headers with customizable colors
   - Proper spacing and typography
   - Collapsible sections with smooth animations

3. **Use Phase 1 AST:**
   - Import `parseToAST` from parser.ts
   - RichTextRenderer should convert AST nodes to React elements

4. **TOC Generation:**
   - Auto-generate from sections with tocId
   - Support custom tocTitle
   - Smooth scroll on click

### Test Requirements

Create `src/renderer/simulator/__tests__/StationpediaRenderer.test.tsx`:
- Test rendering empty device
- Test rendering device with pageDescription
- Test rendering device with operational details
- Test collapsible section toggle
- Test TOC generation
- Test nested operational details

### Implementation Notes

- Use React functional components with TypeScript
- Use CSS modules or plain CSS file
- The renderer will be used in both the main editor window (preview panel) and the simulator window
- Focus on correctness first, pixel-perfect later

### DO NOT:
- Proceed to Phase 3
- Create phase completion files
- Stop for user input

Work autonomously and return a summary of what was implemented when complete.
```

## Phase 2 Complete: Stationpedia Renderer (HTML)

Built an HTML/CSS renderer matching in-game Stationpedia appearance. The renderer takes DeviceDocument objects and renders them with collapsible sections, TOC, logic tables, and full TMP rich text support.

**Files created/changed:**
- src/renderer/simulator/StationpediaRenderer.tsx
- src/renderer/simulator/components/CollapsibleSection.tsx
- src/renderer/simulator/components/DeviceHeader.tsx
- src/renderer/simulator/components/LogicSection.tsx
- src/renderer/simulator/components/OperationalDetailSection.tsx
- src/renderer/simulator/components/RichTextRenderer.tsx
- src/renderer/simulator/components/TOCPanel.tsx
- src/renderer/simulator/styles/stationpedia.css
- src/renderer/simulator/index.ts
- src/renderer/simulator/__tests__/StationpediaRenderer.test.tsx

**Functions created/changed:**
- StationpediaRenderer: Main component rendering full DeviceDocument
- CollapsibleSection: Expand/collapse with depth-based styling
- DeviceHeader: Device name with title color support
- LogicSection: Table-formatted logic descriptions
- OperationalDetailSection: Recursive section rendering with items/steps/children
- RichTextRenderer: AST nodes → React elements with link click handling
- TOCPanel: Auto-generated from tocId fields with smooth scroll

**Tests created/changed:**
- StationpediaRenderer.test.tsx: 30 tests covering all DeviceDocument fields

**Review Status:** APPROVED

**Key Implementation Details:**
1. **Dark theme** matching game (#1a1a2e background, cyan accents)
2. **All device fields rendered**: pageDescription, operationalDetails, logicDescriptions, modeDescriptions, slotDescriptions, versionDescriptions, memoryDescriptions
3. **Rich text via AST**: RichTextRenderer uses parseToAST from Phase 1
4. **Collapsible sections** with smooth transitions and depth-based styling
5. **TOC generation** from operational details with tocId
6. **Responsive design** for various screen sizes

## Phase 3: Editor UI with Drag-and-Drop

You are implementing Phase 3 of the Stationpedia Ascended IDE. Work autonomously to completion.

### Objective
Build the authoring IDE with dockable panels, content tree with drag-and-drop, rich text editor, and property inspector.

### Context
- Workspace: `c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaEditor`
- Phase 1 complete: AST types in `src/renderer/models/ast.ts`, content model in `src/renderer/models/contentModel.ts`
- Phase 2 complete: Renderer in `src/renderer/simulator/`
- Installed packages: react-mosaic-component, @dnd-kit/core, @dnd-kit/sortable, @dnd-kit/utilities, zustand, TipTap

### Files to Create/Modify

1. **`src/renderer/store/editorStore.ts`**
   - Zustand store for editor state
   - Current workspace (WorkspaceModel)
   - Selected device/item
   - Dirty flag for unsaved changes
   - Actions: setWorkspace, selectDevice, updateDevice, addOperationalDetail, removeOperationalDetail, reorderOperationalDetails

2. **`src/renderer/editor/PanelSystem.tsx`**
   - Use react-mosaic-component for dockable panels
   - Default layout: ContentTree (left), Editor (center), Preview (right)
   - Allow panel resize and rearrangement
   - Persist layout to localStorage

3. **`src/renderer/editor/ContentTree.tsx`**
   - Tree view showing all devices/guides in workspace
   - Use react-window for virtualization (already installed)
   - Search/filter functionality
   - Click to select, expands to show operational details

4. **`src/renderer/editor/OperationalDetailsTree.tsx`**
   - Tree showing operational details for selected device
   - Use @dnd-kit/sortable for drag-and-drop reordering
   - Nested sections with proper indentation
   - Drag handles that move entire node + children
   - Add/delete buttons for sections

5. **`src/renderer/editor/RichTextEditor.tsx`**
   - TipTap-based rich text editor
   - Custom toolbar: bold, italic, underline, strikethrough, color, link
   - On change: parse to AST, update store
   - On load: serialize AST to TipTap format

6. **`src/renderer/editor/PropertyInspector.tsx`**
   - Edit selected operational detail properties
   - Fields: title, tocId, collapsible, backgroundColor, imageFile, youtubeUrl
   - For device: displayName, pageDescription, generateToc, tocTitle, colors

7. **`src/renderer/editor/EditorApp.tsx`**
   - Main editor application component
   - Integrates PanelSystem with all editor components
   - Toolbar with File menu (open, save)

8. **`src/renderer/editor/Toolbar.tsx`**
   - Application toolbar
   - File operations (Open Workspace, Save)
   - Mode toggle placeholder (for Phase 4)

9. **`src/renderer/editor/index.ts`**
   - Export all editor components

10. **`src/renderer/editor/styles/editor.css`**
    - Editor-specific styles
    - Dark theme consistent with Stationpedia
    - Panel styling, tree styling

### Key Requirements

1. **react-mosaic for dockable panels:**
   ```tsx
   import { Mosaic, MosaicWindow } from 'react-mosaic-component';
   import 'react-mosaic-component/react-mosaic-component.css';
   ```

2. **@dnd-kit for drag-and-drop:**
   ```tsx
   import { DndContext, closestCenter } from '@dnd-kit/core';
   import { SortableContext, verticalListSortingStrategy, useSortable } from '@dnd-kit/sortable';
   import { CSS } from '@dnd-kit/utilities';
   ```

3. **Zustand store pattern:**
   ```tsx
   import { create } from 'zustand';
   
   interface EditorState {
     workspace: WorkspaceModel | null;
     selectedDeviceKey: string | null;
     // ... actions
   }
   ```

4. **TipTap integration with AST:**
   - Create custom TipTap extension or use content parsing
   - On editor change: serialize to HTML, parse to TMP, then to AST
   - On load: serialize AST to TMP, set as TipTap content

5. **Dark theme consistent with simulator:**
   - Use same color variables as stationpedia.css
   - #1a1a2e background, cyan accents

### Test Requirements

Create `src/renderer/editor/__tests__/`:
- `editorStore.test.ts`: Test store actions
- `OperationalDetailsTree.test.tsx`: Test drag-and-drop reordering
- `ContentTree.test.tsx`: Test selection and filtering

### Implementation Notes

- Focus on functionality first, polish later
- Ensure drag-and-drop works for nested operational details
- The preview panel should use StationpediaRenderer from Phase 2
- Store should emit change events for the preview to update

### DO NOT:
- Proceed to Phase 4
- Create phase completion files
- Stop for user input

Work autonomously and return a summary of what was implemented when complete.
```

## Phase 3 Complete: Editor UI with Drag-and-Drop

Built the authoring IDE with dockable panels using react-mosaic, drag-and-drop operational details tree using @dnd-kit, rich text editing with TipTap, and comprehensive state management with Zustand.

**Files created/changed:**
- src/renderer/store/editorStore.ts
- src/renderer/editor/PanelSystem.tsx
- src/renderer/editor/ContentTree.tsx
- src/renderer/editor/OperationalDetailsTree.tsx
- src/renderer/editor/RichTextEditor.tsx
- src/renderer/editor/PageDescriptionEditor.tsx
- src/renderer/editor/PropertyInspector.tsx
- src/renderer/editor/Toolbar.tsx
- src/renderer/editor/EditorApp.tsx
- src/renderer/editor/styles/editor.css
- src/renderer/editor/index.ts
- src/renderer/editor/__tests__/editorStore.test.ts
- src/renderer/editor/__tests__/ContentTree.test.tsx
- src/renderer/editor/__tests__/OperationalDetailsTree.test.tsx
- vitest.setup.ts

**Functions created/changed:**
- editorStore: Zustand store with workspace, selection, dirty flag, device CRUD operations
- PanelSystem: react-mosaic dockable panels with localStorage persistence
- ContentTree: Device/guide tree with Fuse.js search, expandable nodes
- OperationalDetailsTree: @dnd-kit sortable tree with nested support
- RichTextEditor: TipTap editor with formatting toolbar
- PageDescriptionEditor: Focused page description editing
- PropertyInspector: Dynamic property forms for devices and details
- Toolbar: File operations (Open, Save, Close)
- EditorApp: Main app integrating all components with keyboard shortcuts

**Tests created/changed:**
- editorStore.test.ts: 17 tests for store actions
- ContentTree.test.tsx: 7 tests for tree navigation
- OperationalDetailsTree.test.tsx: 7 tests for drag-and-drop

**Review Status:** APPROVED

**Key Implementation Details:**
1. **react-mosaic** for dockable three-panel layout (Tree/Editor/Preview)
2. **@dnd-kit** for smooth drag-and-drop of operational details with nested support
3. **TipTap** rich text editor with formatting toolbar
4. **Fuse.js** powered search/filter in content tree
5. **Zustand** state management with dirty flag tracking
6. **Dark theme** consistent with Stationpedia styling
7. **Keyboard shortcuts** (Ctrl+S save, Ctrl+O open)
8. **Layout persistence** to localStorage

## Phase 4: Simulator Window

You are implementing Phase 4 of the Stationpedia Ascended IDE. Work autonomously to completion.

### Objective
Create a separate Electron preview window (Simulator) with navigation and IPC-based state synchronization with the main editor window.

### Context
- Workspace: `c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaEditor`
- Phase 1-3 complete with AST, renderer, and editor UI
- The Simulator should show a live preview of the currently selected device
- Communication between windows must use Electron IPC

### Files to Create/Modify

1. **`src/main/simulatorWindow.ts`**
   - Create and manage the Simulator BrowserWindow
   - Handle IPC for state sync
   - Position near main window

2. **`src/main/index.ts`** (modify existing)
   - Add IPC handlers for simulator communication
   - Create simulator window on demand
   - Handle window lifecycle

3. **`src/preload/simulatorPreload.ts`**
   - Preload script for simulator window
   - Expose IPC methods for state sync

4. **`src/renderer/simulator/SimulatorApp.tsx`**
   - Main simulator window application
   - Subscribe to state changes via IPC
   - Display StationpediaRenderer with current device

5. **`src/renderer/simulator/NavigationBar.tsx`**
   - Device navigation within simulator
   - Back/forward history
   - Device selector dropdown
   - Mode toggle (Vanilla ↔ Ascended)

6. **`src/renderer/simulator/simulatorMain.tsx`**
   - Entry point for simulator window
   - Render SimulatorApp

7. **`src/renderer/simulator/simulator.html`**
   - HTML template for simulator window

8. **`src/renderer/services/sharedState.ts`**
   - IPC-based state synchronization
   - Shared state type definitions
   - Subscribe/publish pattern

9. **`src/main/ipcHandlers.ts`**
   - Define all IPC channel names
   - Handler implementations for:
     - Request current device
     - Navigate to device
     - Mode toggle
     - Workspace sync

### Key Requirements

1. **Electron IPC Pattern:**
   ```typescript
   // In main process
   ipcMain.handle('get-current-device', async () => {
     return currentState.device;
   });
   
   ipcMain.on('navigate-to-device', (event, deviceKey) => {
     // Broadcast to all windows
     BrowserWindow.getAllWindows().forEach(win => {
       win.webContents.send('device-changed', deviceKey);
     });
   });
   
   // In preload
   contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld('simulator', {
     getCurrentDevice: () => ipcRenderer.invoke('get-current-device'),
     onDeviceChanged: (callback) => ipcRenderer.on('device-changed', callback),
     navigateToDevice: (key) => ipcRenderer.send('navigate-to-device', key),
   });
   ```

2. **Mode Toggle (Vanilla ↔ Ascended):**
   - Vanilla mode: Show only base game data (hide custom overrides)
   - Ascended mode: Show full content including mod additions
   - Store preference in localStorage

3. **Navigation History:**
   - Track visited devices
   - Back/forward buttons work like browser
   - Deep linking support (could click {THING:} links)

4. **Window Management:**
   - Simulator window opens when user clicks "Open Simulator" or via menu
   - Close simulator closes just that window, not the app
   - Main window closing should close simulator too

### Vite Configuration Update

Update `vite.config.ts` to build simulator entry point:
```typescript
// Add simulator as additional entry
rollupOptions: {
  input: {
    main: 'src/renderer/index.html',
    simulator: 'src/renderer/simulator/simulator.html'
  }
}
```

### Test Requirements

Create `src/renderer/simulator/__tests__/`:
- `SimulatorApp.test.tsx`: Test device rendering
- `NavigationBar.test.tsx`: Test navigation

Create `src/main/__tests__/`:
- `ipcHandlers.test.ts`: Test IPC handlers (if testable)

### Implementation Notes

- The simulator window is optional - app works without it
- IPC is the authoritative sync layer, not direct state sharing
- Simulator should handle being opened when no workspace is loaded
- Use existing StationpediaRenderer from Phase 2

### DO NOT:
- Proceed to Phase 5
- Create phase completion files
- Stop for user input

Work autonomously and return a summary of what was implemented when complete.
```

## Phase 5: Tooltip Editing System

You are implementing Phase 5 of the Stationpedia Ascended IDE. Work autonomously to completion.

### Objective
Add support for editing genericDescriptions (tooltips) that appear when hovering over items in the game. These are stored as a separate section in the JSON and need their own editing UI.

### Context
- Workspace: `c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaEditor`
- Phase 1-4 complete with AST, renderer, editor UI, and simulator window
- The mod's descriptions.json has a "genericDescriptions" section with key-value tooltip definitions
- Example from the mod:
  ```json
  "genericDescriptions": {
    "ItemBattery": "Standard rechargeable power cell...",
    "ItemGasCanister": "Portable gas storage container..."
  }
  ```

### Files to Create/Modify

1. **`src/renderer/models/tooltipModel.ts`**
   - TooltipDefinition type
   - TooltipCollection type
   - Helper functions for tooltip operations

2. **`src/renderer/store/tooltipStore.ts`**
   - Zustand store for tooltip state
   - CRUD operations for tooltips
   - Search/filter functionality
   - Dirty flag tracking

3. **`src/renderer/editor/TooltipEditor.tsx`**
   - Main tooltip editing panel
   - List of all tooltips with search
   - Select tooltip to edit
   - Add new tooltip
   - Delete tooltip

4. **`src/renderer/editor/TooltipItemEditor.tsx`**
   - Edit a single tooltip
   - Key (item identifier) - usually readonly after creation
   - Value (description text) - rich text with TMP support
   - Preview of how it will render

5. **`src/renderer/simulator/TooltipPreview.tsx`**
   - Preview component for tooltips
   - Shows tooltip as it would appear in-game
   - Dark background, styled text

6. **`src/renderer/editor/PanelSystem.tsx`** (modify)
   - Add Tooltips panel as an optional panel type
   - Allow user to show/hide tooltip panel

7. **`src/renderer/services/jsonCodec.ts`** (modify)
   - Ensure genericDescriptions are properly parsed and serialized
   - Preserve unknown keys

8. **`src/renderer/editor/EditorApp.tsx`** (modify)
   - Add tooltips panel integration
   - Add menu/toolbar option to show tooltips

### Key Requirements

1. **Tooltip Structure:**
   ```typescript
   interface TooltipDefinition {
     key: string;           // e.g., "ItemBattery"
     description: string;   // TMP formatted text
     category?: string;     // For organization (Items, Structures, etc.)
   }
   
   interface TooltipCollection {
     tooltips: Map<string, TooltipDefinition>;
     categories: string[];
   }
   ```

2. **Category Organization:**
   - Auto-detect categories from key prefixes (Item*, Structure*, Kit*, etc.)
   - Allow filtering by category
   - Collapsible category groups in list

3. **Rich Text Support:**
   - Use same RichTextEditor from Phase 3
   - Parse existing TMP to AST for editing
   - Serialize back to TMP on save

4. **Search and Filter:**
   - Search by key name
   - Search within description text
   - Filter by category

5. **Preview Integration:**
   - Live preview as user types
   - Tooltip style preview (small popup style)

### Test Requirements

Create tests in `src/renderer/editor/__tests__/`:
- `tooltipStore.test.ts`: Test store CRUD operations
- `TooltipEditor.test.tsx`: Test list and selection
- `TooltipItemEditor.test.tsx`: Test editing

Create tests in `src/renderer/simulator/__tests__/`:
- `TooltipPreview.test.tsx`: Test rendering

### Implementation Notes

- Tooltips are simpler than device pages - just key + description
- Keep the UI consistent with device editing
- The tooltip panel should be hideable to not clutter the UI
- Consider bulk operations (import/export CSV?)

### DO NOT:
- Proceed to Phase 6
- Create phase completion files
- Stop for user input

Work autonomously and return a summary of what was implemented when complete.
```

## Phase 6: Validation & Assets

You are implementing Phase 6 of the Stationpedia Ascended IDE. Work autonomously to completion.

### Objective
Add comprehensive content validation and asset management (images, icons) for the Stationpedia editor.

### Context
- Workspace: `c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaEditor`
- Phase 1-5 complete with AST, renderer, editor UI, simulator window, and tooltip editing
- The mod references images via imageFile fields and uses game icons
- Content needs validation for broken links, missing assets, TMP syntax errors

### Files to Create/Modify

1. **`src/renderer/services/validator.ts`**
   - Content validation rules
   - Validate TMP syntax (unclosed tags, invalid colors)
   - Validate internal links ({THING:} references exist)
   - Validate image references (file exists)
   - Validate required fields (deviceKey, title on details)
   - Return structured validation results

2. **`src/renderer/models/validationModel.ts`**
   - ValidationResult type
   - ValidationError type (error, warning, info levels)
   - ValidationContext type

3. **`src/renderer/editor/ValidationPanel.tsx`**
   - Display validation errors/warnings
   - Click to navigate to error location
   - Filter by severity
   - Auto-run validation on content change (debounced)

4. **`src/renderer/editor/AssetBrowser.tsx`**
   - Browse available images in workspace
   - Preview images
   - Insert image reference into editor
   - Show which images are used/unused

5. **`src/renderer/editor/AssetPreview.tsx`**
   - Preview component for images
   - Thumbnail grid view
   - Full preview on click

6. **`src/renderer/services/assetService.ts`**
   - Scan workspace for assets (png, jpg)
   - Track asset usage
   - Resolve asset paths

7. **`src/renderer/editor/PanelSystem.tsx`** (modify)
   - Add Validation panel type
   - Add Assets panel type

8. **`src/renderer/store/validationStore.ts`**
   - Store validation results
   - Track which items have errors
   - Validation status (running, complete, error)

### Key Requirements

1. **Validation Rules:**
   ```typescript
   interface ValidationRule {
     id: string;
     name: string;
     severity: 'error' | 'warning' | 'info';
     validate: (context: ValidationContext) => ValidationError[];
   }
   
   // Example rules:
   // - unclosed-tag: TMP tag not closed (<b> without </b>)
   // - invalid-color: Invalid hex color format
   // - broken-link: {THING:X} where X doesn't exist
   // - missing-asset: imageFile references non-existent file
   // - empty-title: OperationalDetail without title
   // - duplicate-tocId: Multiple sections with same tocId
   ```

2. **Validation Result:**
   ```typescript
   interface ValidationError {
     ruleId: string;
     severity: 'error' | 'warning' | 'info';
     message: string;
     location: {
       deviceKey?: string;
       field?: string;
       sectionIndex?: number;
       line?: number;
       column?: number;
     };
   }
   ```

3. **Asset Browser Features:**
   - Grid view of available images
   - Drag-and-drop to insert image reference
   - Show usage count per image
   - Filter by folder/type

4. **Integration with Editor:**
   - Validation errors show inline markers in editor
   - Click error to jump to location
   - Status bar shows error count

### Test Requirements

Create tests in `src/renderer/services/__tests__/`:
- `validator.test.ts`: Test all validation rules

Create tests in `src/renderer/editor/__tests__/`:
- `ValidationPanel.test.tsx`: Test error display and navigation
- `AssetBrowser.test.tsx`: Test asset browsing

Create tests in `src/renderer/store/__tests__/`:
- `validationStore.test.ts`: Test store operations

### Implementation Notes

- Validation should be fast (run on every change with debounce)
- Cache validation results per device
- Asset scanning can be slow - do it once on workspace load
- Consider lazy loading asset thumbnails

### DO NOT:
- Proceed to Phase 7
- Create phase completion files
- Stop for user input

Work autonomously and return a summary of what was implemented when complete.
```

## Phase 7: Polish & Persistence

You are implementing Phase 7 (final phase) of the Stationpedia Ascended IDE. Work autonomously to completion.

### Objective
Add final polish, file persistence, and user documentation to complete the IDE.

### Context
- Workspace: `c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaEditor`
- Phase 1-6 complete with full editor functionality
- This phase focuses on:
  1. File I/O (open/save workspace files)
  2. Auto-save and backup
  3. Keyboard shortcuts
  4. User documentation (README, usage guide)
  5. Error handling polish

### Files to Create/Modify

1. **`src/renderer/services/persistence.ts`**
   - File open/save operations
   - Recent files tracking
   - Auto-save with configurable interval
   - Backup before save
   - Dirty state detection

2. **`src/renderer/services/fileDialogs.ts`**
   - Native file dialog integration via Electron
   - Open workspace dialog
   - Save/Save As dialogs
   - Confirm unsaved changes dialog

3. **`src/main/fileHandlers.ts`**
   - Main process file operations
   - IPC handlers for file dialogs
   - File reading/writing with error handling

4. **`src/renderer/hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts.ts`**
   - Global keyboard shortcut handler
   - Ctrl+S (Save), Ctrl+O (Open), Ctrl+Z (Undo), Ctrl+Y (Redo)
   - Ctrl+Shift+S (Save As), Ctrl+N (New)
   - F5 (Refresh preview)

5. **`src/renderer/editor/StatusBar.tsx`**
   - Status bar at bottom of editor
   - Show file path, dirty state, validation summary
   - Show character/word count for selected field

6. **`src/renderer/editor/RecentFilesMenu.tsx`**
   - Recent files dropdown
   - Clear recent files option
   - Pin frequently used files

7. **`src/renderer/components/ConfirmDialog.tsx`**
   - Reusable confirmation dialog
   - Unsaved changes warning
   - Delete confirmation

8. **`src/renderer/editor/EditorApp.tsx`** (modify)
   - Integrate persistence service
   - Add status bar
   - Connect keyboard shortcuts
   - Handle app close with unsaved changes

9. **`README.md`** (update or create in StationpediaEditor folder)
   - Installation instructions
   - Feature overview
   - Keyboard shortcuts reference
   - Workflow guide

10. **`docs/USER_GUIDE.md`**
    - Comprehensive user guide
    - Getting started
    - Panel descriptions
    - Editing workflow
    - Tips and tricks

### Key Requirements

1. **File Persistence:**
   ```typescript
   interface PersistenceService {
     openWorkspace(): Promise<WorkspaceModel>;
     saveWorkspace(workspace: WorkspaceModel): Promise<void>;
     saveWorkspaceAs(workspace: WorkspaceModel): Promise<string>;
     hasUnsavedChanges(): boolean;
     getRecentFiles(): string[];
     addRecentFile(path: string): void;
   }
   ```

2. **Auto-save:**
   - Configurable interval (default 30 seconds)
   - Only save if dirty
   - Save to temp file first, then move to target

3. **Backup:**
   - Create .bak file before overwriting
   - Keep last 3 backups

4. **Keyboard Shortcuts:**
   - Documented in status bar tooltip
   - Configurable (future enhancement)
   - No conflicts with text editing

5. **Error Handling:**
   - Toast notifications for errors
   - Graceful degradation
   - Clear error messages

### Test Requirements

Create tests in `src/renderer/services/__tests__/`:
- `persistence.test.ts`: Test save/load operations

Create tests in `src/renderer/hooks/__tests__/`:
- `useKeyboardShortcuts.test.ts`: Test shortcut handling

Create tests in `src/renderer/editor/__tests__/`:
- `StatusBar.test.tsx`: Test status display

### Documentation Requirements

README.md should include:
- Project description
- Features list
- Installation
- Building from source
- Keyboard shortcuts table
- Contributing guidelines

USER_GUIDE.md should include:
- Getting started tutorial
- UI overview with panel descriptions
- Editing devices walkthrough
- Working with tooltips
- Validation workflow
- Best practices

### DO NOT:
- Create phase completion files (I will do that)
- Stop for user input

Work autonomously and return a summary of what was implemented when complete.

## Phase 7 Complete: Polish & Persistence

Built file persistence, auto-save, keyboard shortcuts, status bar, recent files menu, and comprehensive user documentation to complete the IDE.

**Files created/changed:**

- src/renderer/services/__tests__/persistence.test.ts

- useKeyboardShortcuts: Global hook for Ctrl+S, Ctrl+O, Ctrl+Shift+S, Ctrl+N, Ctrl+W, F5
- ConfirmDialog: Reusable modal with title, message, confirm/cancel buttons, danger mode
- StatusBar: File path, dirty indicator, validation summary, keyboard shortcuts help
- RecentFilesMenu: Dropdown with recent files, pin support, clear option

**Tests created/changed:**
- persistence.test.ts: 11 tests for file operations
- useKeyboardShortcuts.test.ts: 7 tests for shortcut handling
- StatusBar.test.tsx: 11 tests for status display

## Plan Complete: Stationpedia Ascended IDE

Built a comprehensive desktop IDE for authoring and validating the Stationeers mod "Stationpedia Ascended" with lossless JSON round-tripping, HTML-based Stationpedia rendering, dual-window architecture, and professional editing features.

**Phases Completed:** 7 of 7
1. ✅ Phase 1: Content AST & Lossless Serialization
2. ✅ Phase 2: Stationpedia Renderer (HTML)
3. ✅ Phase 3: Editor UI with Drag-and-Drop
4. ✅ Phase 4: Simulator Window
5. ✅ Phase 5: Tooltip Editing System
6. ✅ Phase 6: Validation & Assets
7. ✅ Phase 7: Polish & Persistence

**Key Functions/Classes Added:**
- AST Types: 13 node types for TMP rich text
- Parser: parseToAST for TMP string parsing
- Serializer: serializeToTMP with originalFormat preservation
- JSON Codec: Lossless round-trip with field normalization
- StationpediaRenderer: Full device rendering to HTML
- EditorStore: Zustand state management
- TooltipStore: Tooltip editing state
- ValidationStore: Validation results caching
- PersistenceService: File I/O with auto-save
- Validator: 8+ validation rules
- AssetService: Asset tracking and usage

**Test Coverage:**

- ✅ Drag-and-drop operational details with @dnd-kit
- ✅ TipTap rich text editing

_**User (2026-01-17 00:45:19Z)**_

Ok how do I test it?

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.5)**_

Great question! Here's how to test the Stationpedia Editor IDE:

## Quick Start

### 1. **Run the Development Version**

```powershell
cd "c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaEditor"
npm run dev
```

This will:
- Start the Vite dev server
- Launch the Electron window automatically
- Enable hot-reload for development

### 2. **What You'll See**

When the app launches, you should see:
- **Welcome Screen** with "Open Workspace" and "Recent Files" (if any)
- **Menu/Toolbar** at the top
- The IDE will be waiting for you to load a workspace

### 3. **Test Workflows**

#### **A. Load the Mod's descriptions.json**
```powershell
# The mod's actual data file is at:
# c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaAscended\mod\descriptions.json
```

1. Click **"Open Workspace"** button
2. Navigate to `StationpediaAscended\mod\`
3. Select `descriptions.json`
4. The IDE should load all devices, guides, and tooltips

#### **B. Try the Editor Features**

**Content Tree (Left Panel):**
- See all devices listed
- Search for "Solar" or "Battery"
- Click a device to select it

**Editor Panel (Center):**
- Edit the page description (rich text)
- Add/remove operational details
- Drag to reorder sections

**Preview Panel (Right):**
- See live preview of your changes
- Should match in-game appearance

**Tooltips Tab:**
- Click "Show Tooltips" button in toolbar
- Browse genericDescriptions
- Edit tooltip text

**Validation Tab:**
- Click "Show Validation" if available
- See any TMP syntax errors
- Click errors to navigate to them

#### **C. Test the Simulator Window**

1. Click **"Open Simulator"** (if there's a button) or check the menu
2. A second window should open
3. Navigate between devices
4. Toggle Vanilla ↔ Ascended modes
5. Changes in main editor should sync to simulator

#### **D. Test Keyboard Shortcuts**

- **Ctrl+S** - Save workspace
- **Ctrl+O** - Open workspace
- **Ctrl+Shift+S** - Save As
- **F5** - Refresh preview

#### **E. Test Auto-Save**

1. Make a change to a device
2. Wait 30 seconds
3. Check if the file was auto-saved (dirty indicator should clear)

### 4. **Check the Logs**

Open Developer Tools to see any errors:
- Press **Ctrl+Shift+I** in the Electron window
- Check Console tab for errors/warnings

### 5. **Run Tests**

To verify everything works:
```powershell
npm test
```

Should show: **422/422 tests passing ✅**

### 6. **Build Production Version** (Optional)

```powershell
npm run build
```

This creates a distributable app in the `release/` folder.

---

## Troubleshooting

**If the window doesn't open:**
- Check terminal for errors
- Try: `npm run type-check` to verify TypeScript is clean
- Look for port conflicts (Vite uses port 5173 by default)

**If JSON doesn't load:**
- Check console for parsing errors
- Verify the JSON is valid
- The file should have a `devices` array at minimum

**If preview doesn't render:**
- Check if AST parsing is working (console logs)
- Verify TMP tags are being parsed correctly

Let me know what happens when you try to run it! 🚀

---

```
# Stationpedia Editor - Quick Start

## Running the Editor

### Development Mode
```powershell
# Option 1: Use the batch file
.\start-dev.bat

# Option 2: Use npm directly
npm run dev
```

This will:
- Start Vite dev server on port 5173
- Launch Electron window automatically
- Enable hot-reload for development
- Open DevTools for debugging

### Production Build
```powershell
npm run build
```

Creates executable in `release/` folder.

## First Time Setup

1. **Open a Workspace**
   - Click "Open Workspace" button
   - Navigate to: `StationpediaAscended\mod\`
   - Select `descriptions.json`

2. **Explore the Interface**
   - **Left Panel**: Content Tree (devices, guides, tooltips)
   - **Center Panel**: Editor and Operational Details
   - **Right Panel**: Live Preview
   - **Toggle Panels**: Validation, Assets, Tooltips via toolbar

3. **Make Edits**
   - Select a device from Content Tree
   - Edit page description with rich text toolbar
   - Add/remove operational details sections
   - Drag to reorder sections
   - Auto-save happens every 30 seconds

## Keyboard Shortcuts

| Shortcut | Action |
|----------|--------|
| `Ctrl+S` | Save workspace |
| `Ctrl+O` | Open workspace |
| `Ctrl+Shift+S` | Save As |
| `Ctrl+N` | New workspace |
| `Ctrl+W` | Close workspace |
| `F5` | Refresh preview |

## Testing

```powershell
# Run all tests (422 tests)
npm test

# Run tests with UI
npm run test:ui

# Type checking
npm run type-check
```

## Project Structure

```
src/
├── main/               # Electron main process
├── preload/            # Electron preload scripts
└── renderer/           # React application
    ├── editor/         # Editor UI components
    ├── simulator/      # Preview renderer
    ├── components/     # Shared components
    ├── models/         # TypeScript types
    ├── services/       # Business logic
    ├── store/          # Zustand state management
    └── hooks/          # React hooks
```

## Troubleshooting

**Window doesn't open**
- Check terminal for errors
- Verify node_modules installed: `npm install`
- Try: `npm run type-check`

**JSON doesn't load**
- Check console for parsing errors
- Verify JSON is valid
- File must have `devices` array

**Preview not rendering**
- Open DevTools (Ctrl+Shift+I)
- Check Console tab for errors
- Verify TMP tags are valid

## Documentation

- Full user guide: `docs/USER_GUIDE.md`
- README: `README.md`
- Phase completion docs: `plans/`

```

- filePath: `c:\Dev\12-17-25 Stationeers Respawn Update Code\StationpediaEditor\QUICK_START.md`

# Cleanup Summary

## Removed Old WPF/C# Project Files

The following old files and directories from the previous WPF version were removed:

### Files:
- `App.xaml` - WPF application definition
- `App.xaml.cs` - WPF application code-behind
- `build-exe.bat` - Old build script
- `launch.bat` - Old launcher
- `StationpediaEditor.csproj` - C# project file

### Directories:
- `Converters/` - WPF value converters
- `Models/` - Old C# models (replaced by TypeScript models)
- `Services/` - Old C# services (replaced by TypeScript services)
- `ViewModels/` - WPF ViewModels (no longer needed with React)
- `Views/` - WPF XAML views (replaced by React components)
- `bin/` - C# build output
- `obj/` - C# build intermediates
- `assets/` - Old WPF assets

### Old Documentation:
- `BUILD.md` - Old build instructions
- `ELECTRON_README.md` - Old Electron notes
- `QUICK_REFERENCE.md` - Outdated reference
- `PHASE_*.md` files in root (moved to `plans/`)

## Removed Old React Components

The following old React components were replaced by the new Phase 3-7 implementation:

### Removed:
- `src/renderer/App.tsx` - Old app entry point
- `src/renderer/components/Editor.tsx` - Replaced by `editor/RichTextEditor.tsx` and `editor/PageDescriptionEditor.tsx`
- `src/renderer/components/Preview.tsx` - Replaced by `simulator/StationpediaRenderer.tsx`
- `src/renderer/components/Sidebar.tsx` - Replaced by `editor/ContentTree.tsx`
- `src/renderer/components/OperationalDetailsEditor.tsx` - Replaced by `editor/OperationalDetailsTree.tsx`
- `src/renderer/components/LinkPickerModal.tsx` - No longer needed
- `src/renderer/components/ValidationPanel.tsx` (old) - Replaced by `editor/ValidationPanel.tsx`

### Kept:
- `src/renderer/components/ConfirmDialog.tsx` - Still in use by EditorApp
- `src/renderer/components/LoadingSpinner.tsx` - Utility component

## New Structure

The project now has a clean, organized structure:

```
StationpediaEditor/
├── src/
│   ├── main/              # Electron main process
│   ├── preload/           # Electron preload scripts
│   └── renderer/          # React application
│       ├── editor/        # Editor UI (NEW - Phase 3)
│       ├── simulator/     # Preview renderer (NEW - Phase 2)
│       ├── components/    # Shared components (CLEANED)
│       ├── models/        # TypeScript models (Phase 1)
│       ├── services/      # Business logic (Phases 1-7)
│       ├── store/         # State management (Phases 3-6)
│       └── hooks/         # React hooks (Phase 7)
├── docs/                  # Documentation
├── plans/                 # Implementation plans
├── start-dev.bat         # NEW - Easy launcher
├── QUICK_START.md        # NEW - Quick reference
└── README.md             # Updated

```

## Ready to Use

The editor is now ready to run with:
```powershell
.\start-dev.bat
```

All 422 tests pass ✅  
TypeScript compiles cleanly ✅  
Old files removed ✅

```
/**
 * Preload Script - Exposes safe IPC methods to renderer
 */
const { contextBridge, ipcRenderer } = require('electron');

// Expose file system API
contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld('electronAPI', {
  // Dialog
  openFolder: () => ipcRenderer.invoke('dialog:openFolder'),

  // File system
  readFile: (filePath: string) => ipcRenderer.invoke('fs:readFile', filePath),
  writeFile: (filePath: string, content: string, createBackup: boolean = true) =>
    ipcRenderer.invoke('fs:writeFile', filePath, content, createBackup),
  listFiles: (directory: string, extensions: string[] = []) =>
    ipcRenderer.invoke('fs:listFiles', directory, extensions),
  exists: (filePath: string) => ipcRenderer.invoke('fs:exists', filePath),
  stat: (filePath: string) => ipcRenderer.invoke('fs:stat', filePath),

  // Settings
  getSettings: () => ipcRenderer.invoke('settings:get'),
  setSettings: (settings: object) => ipcRenderer.invoke('settings:set', settings),
});
```

- oldString:

```
/**
 * Preload Script - Exposes safe IPC methods to renderer
 */
import { contextBridge, ipcRenderer } from 'electron';

// Expose file system API
contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld('electronAPI', {
  // Dialog
  openFolder: () => ipcRenderer.invoke('dialog:openFolder'),

  // File system
  readFile: (filePath: string) => ipcRenderer.invoke('fs:readFile', filePath),
  writeFile: (filePath: string, content: string, createBackup: boolean = true) =>
    ipcRenderer.invoke('fs:writeFile', filePath, content, createBackup),
  listFiles: (directory: string, extensions: string[] = []) =>
    ipcRenderer.invoke('fs:listFiles', directory, extensions),
  exists: (filePath: string) => ipcRenderer.invoke('fs:exists', filePath),
  stat: (filePath: string) => ipcRenderer.invoke('fs:stat', filePath),

  // Settings
  getSettings: () => ipcRenderer.invoke('settings:get'),
  setSettings: (settings: object) => ipcRenderer.invoke('settings:set', settings),
});
```
